  • Een aantrekkelijk brutoloon van €2800–€3100
  • Maaltijdcheques van €7 per gewerkte dag
  • Een voltijdse tewerkstelling op de site in Puurs (eigen vervoer vereist)
  • Opstart midden mei
  • Kans op vaste indienstname bij relevante ervaring

Jouw nieuwe werkgever

Ben jij nauwkeurig, analytisch en sterk in data input? Voor een internationale logistieke organisatie in Puurs zoeken we een administratief medewerker data die een sleutelrol speelt in een grootschalige datamigratie tussen twee systemen. Je werkt in een dynamische, groeiende omgeving met een informele cultuur en veel ruimte voor initiatief.


Jouw nieuwe functie

Als administratief medewerker data ben je verantwoordelijk voor:

  • Correct overzetten van zendingen tussen twee systemen (datamigratie/system migration)
  • Uitvoeren van aanpassingen en data‑updates volgens duidelijke werkinstructies
  • Controleren, opsporen en rapporteren van fouten in de data
  • Nauw samenwerken met collega’s om een efficiënte en foutloze data processing te garanderen


Kortom: je bent een belangrijke schakel in een grootschalige systeemovergang en zorgt ervoor dat alle data correct en tijdig verwerkt wordt.


Jouw profiel

Voor deze rol als administratief medewerker data zoeken we iemand die zich herkent in het volgende:

  • Je werkt nauwkeurig, analytisch en gestructureerd
  • Je staat open voor dataverwerking en werkt graag zelfstandig
  • Je hebt een goede kennis van het Engels; Nederlands is een plus
  • Je hebt sterke systeemkennis of ervaring met data‑analyse
  • Je bent beschikbaar voor een tijdelijke opdracht van 4 maanden
  • Ervaring in de logistieke sector en/of eerdere data‑entry ervaring is een troef en kan leiden tot een vast contract


Solliciteer meteen op deze vacature

  • Ben jij de nauwkeurige administratieve medewerker die we zoeken voor deze tijdelijke opdracht? Solliciteer online of neem contact op met onze consultant Arzia Zekir.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
As a functional data analyst at ING you will play a crucial role in the data-driven culture. Your work will empower teams and leadership to make smarter, faster, and more impactful choices.

In This Role, You Will


help shaping needed views/data product to answer business questions efficiently.
Design and optimize Power BI data models to deliver high-performing reports and dashboards that drive clarity and efficiency.
Develop, automate and enhance dashboards, ensuring seamless access to insights that fuel innovation and data-driven decision-making across the organization.
Create compelling visualizations that translate complex data into clear, intuitive stories.


Data Modeling and Collaboration: 


Partner with business stakeholders to deeply understand problem statements and define how data should be transformed, loaded, and presented for maximum impact.
Serve as a strategic link between Business and Data Management. Translating complex business needs into clear data requirements and functional analysis.
Define smart Data Quality Rules in a framework
Test & validate the requested views/data product.
Design and optimize Power BI data models that enable advanced features such as hierarchical filtering and multi-level organizational structures.


Reporting and Automation: 


Contribute to the creation of Center of Expertise for Power BI 
Develop and automate underlying processes needed for regular reports (weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annually). 
Building interactive dashboards/reports that deliver actionable insights.


We Offer You

A clear purpose, a unique offer and a range of flexible compensation and other benefits:


Personal growth & challenging work with endless opportunities to realise your ambitions
An informal, dynamic environment with innovative colleagues supporting your endeavors’
A progressive and agile way of working, where new ideas are valued ahead of convention


A hybrid way of working


We give the autonomy to our employees to let them organize their work in a flexible way that suits best for them while ensuring business continuity, customer service and employee wellbeing.


We redefine banking. What about you?

We’re on a journey that’s centered around our customers, powered by technology and driven by smart, determined people. Our customers feel our people are empowering them to stay a step ahead in life and in business. We’re proud of that!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
In the role of the Data Analyst, you will be responsible for transforming the data into meaningful and actionable insights that support the decision making for B2B stakeholders and management within the EBU department.




Your main responsibilities:

o Design, build and run dashboards allowing B2B stakeholders and management to monitor our commercial performance and define potential corrective actions

o Answer business analytics requests and translate them into data requirements:

Identify the “Why” behind each business request
Solve well-defined problems and applies existing methods and tools to business problems
Collect, process, and perform statistical analyses on large datasets by implementing the most appropriate statistical method / data model
Create reports and data visualizations to communicate insights to internal customers
Work on data cleaning, data validation, and exploratory data analysis
Explain the result coming out of the analysis and facilitate the decision making

o Contribute to the overall efficiency by developing a Power BI environment, data marts and procedures that further automate the delivery of recurrent reports and analyses, from data extraction to visualization.

o Contribute in improving structurally the quality and the freshness of the data sources by communicating quality issues to data engineers and data governance manager and monitoring the progress.

o Suggest new types of data analyses and visualization methods and contribute to the data acculturation of the company.




Your profile:

Diploma in a quantitative area: engineer, commercial engineer, economics, statistics, maths or a related discipline.
Minimum 3 years of professional experience in a commercial department, marketing, or a closely related field
Knowledge of expert data handling techniques and data software languages: R, Python, SQL, Cognos, MS Office (Excel & PowerPoint)
Knowledge in Data visualization and data analysis (Power BI, GCP,...)
Experience in telco and knowledge of B2B market would be a plus
Communication and presentation skills
Ability to work independently in a context of tight deadlines
Fluent English, and French or Dutch




We offer:

You will make the difference in this highly innovative vibe. As a result, you will develop your full potential by working with highly skilled colleagues and through continuous training. Moreover, you can count on a highly competitive and versatile compensation (market competitive salary, company car or legal mobility budget, homeworking and net allowance, performance bonus, meal vouchers) and benefits package.




Inspired? Please send us your resume and we will rapidly get back to you to invite you for further interviews.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Cleaning, Transformation & Standardisation

Clean, structure, and standardise cyber-related datasets submitted by NATO Allies and external research institutions
Ensure data alignment with agreed repository taxonomy, schema, and codebooks
Identify inconsistencies, gaps, and missing information and coordinate corrective actions with stakeholders

Data Validation & Quality Assurance

Perform continuous data quality checks to ensure accuracy, completeness, consistency, and timeliness
Validate data classification, tagging, and compliance with NATO cyber response taxonomy
Support preparation of feedback and reporting to Allies and research partners

Data Integration & Technical Support

Work closely with data engineers to support data ingestion and integration into the NATO repository platform
Assist with testing of upload tools, dashboards, visualisation features, and filtering capabilities
Contribute to metadata management and documentation of data structures

Analytics, Reporting & Dashboards

Produce analytical reports and insights on cyber response trends, patterns, and anomalies
Create dashboards and visual analytics for NATO leadership and committees
Support strategic reporting and decision-making through data-driven analysis

Training & User Support

Support onboarding and training activities for end users
Prepare training materials including guides, presentations, and demonstrations
Provide analytical and technical support during webinars and onboarding sessions

Cyber Defence Pledge (CDP) Support

Support analysis of Allied cyber maturity self-assessments
Conduct comparative trend analysis across reporting cycles
Support automated dashboard and reporting generation for annual CDP activities




Requirements

Experience

Minimum 2 years of experience as a Data Analyst or similar role
Proven experience with:
Data cleaning and transformation
Data analysis and reporting
Dashboard development and visualisation
Data quality assurance
Experience working with multidisciplinary or international teams

Technical Skills

Strong proficiency in Python for data processing and analytics
Experience with data visualisation and dashboard tools
Ability to analyse and structure large datasets
Strong analytical and statistical capabilities
Ability to produce reports and insights for non-technical stakeholders

Preferred Skills

ArcGIS (ArcGIS Online / Experience Builder)
Web application development exposure
Cybersecurity data analysis experience
Experience preparing training materials and supporting end users

Soft Skills

Strong communication and presentation skills
Excellent attention to detail
Ability to manage repetitive analytical tasks with high accuracy
Collaborative and team-oriented mindset

Language Requirements

Fluent English (written and spoken)







Location : Brussels

Only NATO Countries Citizens holding valid NATO SECRET clearance

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Can’t wait to make an impact on the world? You’re not alone. Join us in driving progress in the working world and beyond.

Your journey with us

The Tax Data & Process Team is looking for an experienced consultant to strengthen their team (Data2insight) in a wide range of analytics projects related to strategy, operations, and financial performance within the Tax landscape.

You will take ownership of client workstreams, shape engagement approaches, and act as a trusted advisor on how to turn fragmented tax data into a strategic asset. You will gain exposure to multiple industries with a chance to choose a focus and help to shape Deloitte market offerings. 

Under the direction of a Manager or Lead, you will be:


Managing tax data readiness assessments
Designing end-to-end tax data journeys and identifying control gaps and automation opportunities
Architecting data transformation and migration approaches
Building, configuring and analyzing tax data sets to be used for modeling, clustering, segmentation, and data mining
Delivering analytical insights
Using a variety of technologies for process analysis and design and data analytics
Developing tax dashboard and insight layers that connect operations data to strategic decision-making
Evaluating and recommending AI tools and platforms for tax data analysis and compliance monitoring
Contributing to the development of AI-powered tax insights and advisory offerings
Mentoring junior team members and contributing to offering development, thought leadership, and go-to market strategy.


Let's Talk About You


You have 2 to 5 years of professional experience
Relevant experience in Data Science within corporate Finance and/ or Accounting.
You are a team player with strong communication skills with the ability to tailor your presentation style to different levels of the organization
You have an intellectual curiosity in combination with outstanding analytical skills
You have hands-on experience with data analysis, ETL processes, reconciliation logic, BI/Dashboarding tools etc.
You have a genuine interest / passion in tax technology, regulatory change and the evolving role of data in tax
Familiarity with AI/ML concepts and their application in finance and tax
Understanding of data governance, quality, and ethics in AI-driven solutions
You are structured and process minded
You have a true willingness and ability to work as part of an integrated, enthusiastic and multidisciplinary team
You’re open to work in an international environment and to work abroad if needed
You’re fluent in English. Dutch and/or French is a plus


As a member of our Tax Technology Consulting (TTC) team, you will be part of a team that consists of people with diverse backgrounds. Some of us are data scientists, others are SAP system specialists and others are finance/tax process generalists. What unites us is our mission to solve complex finance/tax problems focusing on processes and data supported with cutting edge technology. Through this we unlock value to our client’s tax teams by bringing efficiencies in their day-to-day processes.

Who is Deloitte?

We provide industry-leading audit and assurance, tax and legal, consulting and related services. We are committed to driving innovation across offerings to help our clients address their challenges, while giving our professionals opportunities to learn and grow in this era of transformation.

In Belgium, +5000 dedicated professionals active in +10 offices, take great pride in bringing multidisciplinary expertise to a wide variety of clients, from national and international companies, small, fast-growing and large organizations to public institutions and governmental authorities.

Why Deloitte?

Be the true you! We foster diversity and inclusion and encourage you to bring your authentic self to work. Explore, question and collaborate while building a career that inspires and energises you.

Never stop growing! Diversity of thought makes us stronger. At Deloitte, we tailor a personalized learning experience, offering you the opportunity to grow at your own pace and achieve maximum impact.

We practice what we preach! As a Purpose-led organisation, at the heart of everything we do is a set of timeless principles and unifying values .

Life looks different for each of us, so we created a varied benefits package that you can tap into:


My Benefits My Choice, a flexible rewards plan tailored to your lifestyle and priorities
Sustainable transport options offered by Mobility@Deloitte
Flexible work arrangements for all and initiatives supported by Parents & Caregivers @Deloitte
Wellbeing tips and activities powered by Energise@Deloitte
Topped off with other health benefits and insurance opportunities


Empowering our employees with flexible work arrangements remains essential in today's reality:


Hybrid workplace: combination of home office and on-site (+10 offices in Belgium or client's premises).
Part-time employment: all our jobs are open to full-time or part-time work under a 90% or 80% regime.


Join us to make an impact together! Apply now!

Title: Data Analyst / Data Visualisation
Description:
Data Analyst / Data Visualisation Consultant

Who We Are

NORRIQ is a leading international consulting firm headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with 350+ consultants driving business transformation through technology. Our Financial Services practice includes 100+ specialists supporting banks, insurers, and financial institutions in navigating regulatory challenges, embracing emerging technologies, and meeting evolving client demands. We prioritize growth, innovation, and excellence, offering abundant opportunities for professional development and collaboration.

The Opportunity

NORRIQ is seeking to grow its Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence team with additional Data Analyst / Data Visualisation Consultants to improve the business performance of financial institutions. You will help our clients to optimize the value they can get from their internal and external data, through improved business performance monitoring and enhanced reporting quality and readability. Join us to work on impactful projects that shape the future of finance.

Responsibilities


Collecting and interpreting internal and external data about customer behaviour via the digital platforms (Web and mobile) and their digital transactions with the bank.
Translate data insight into actionable recommendations in order to coordinate the business needs around data and improve the bank’s digital and marketing strategy and optimize future campaigns.
Collect, analyze, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and correlations
Develop and maintain complex data models, databases, data warehouses and data marts .
Create data visualizations and reports to communicate insights to stakeholders.
Perform data quality checks and ensure data integrity, develop and implement data governance policies and procedures
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and emerging technologies in data analysis and visualization


Your Profile


Master’s degree (or equivalent work experience) in mathematics or statistics, business intelligence, computer engineering, economy.
1-3 years of experience in data projects, preferably within the financial services industry.
Technical Expertise & Tooling: SQL (mandatory), SAS (nice to have), Python & R (to support our AI team), Reporting & Visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, Adobe Analytics, ...),.
ETL Processes: Affinity with ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes, including an understanding of data preparation, cleansing, and integration from multiple sources.
Analytical Skills: Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities to translate complex data into actionable insights.
Business Analysis Skills: Experience in requirements gathering, process design and developing action plans.
AI knowledge: AI knowledge & interest to connect easily with our data scientist and machine learning engineers.
Communication & Collaboration: Excellent communication skills; ability to work well in cross-functional teams and engage effectively with stakeholders.
Languages: Fluent in English and native in French or Dutch.


What We Offer


Dynamic Work Environment: High-impact projects with leading financial institutions in a fast-growing consulting firm.
Career Development: Continuous learning and growth opportunities to advance your career.
Collaborative Culture: A supportive, innovative team environment that encourages new ideas and professional development.
Global Exposure: International experience working on cross-border projects that shape the financial services industry.


Why Join Us?

At NORRIQ, we are shaping the future of finance through transformation and innovation. If you're passionate about improving financial processes, driving change, and delivering value to clients, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

  • Analytical Skills: Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities to translate complex data into actionable insights.
  • Business Analysis Skills: Experience in requirements gathering, process design and developing action plans.
  • AI knowledge: AI knowledge & interest to connect easily with our data scientist and machine learning engineers.
  • Communication & Collaboration: Excellent communication skills; ability to work well in cross-functional teams and engage effectively with stakeholders.
  • Languages: Fluent in English and native in French or Dutch.

What We Offer

  • Dynamic Work Environment: High-impact projects with leading financial institutions in a fast-growing consulting firm.
  • Career Development: Continuous learning and growth opportunities to advance your career.
  • Collaborative Culture: A supportive, innovative team environment that encourages new ideas and professional development.
  • Global Exposure: International experience working on cross-border projects that shape the financial services industry.

Why Join Us?

At NORRIQ, we are shaping the future of finance through transformation and innovation. If you're passionate about improving financial processes, driving change, and delivering value to clients, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description & Summary


Leading with heart, living by values

PwC Advisory is a network of multiple possibilities - where you want to make an impact is always up to you. Working in PwC Advisory services offers a roadmap of possibilities, and you’re in the driver’s seat. With the full support of our Advisory teams, you’ll delve into the inner workings of companies to find the best solutions for any challenge they may be facing. You’ll provide the core expertise and insights required to help transform our client’s business into future-proof organizations. A career within Managed Services, will provide you with the opportunity to help our clients face their big complex and operational problems. We focus on understanding the forces shaping their business and developing industry- tailored solutions that support our clients through the entire lifecycle. As a Senior associate within the Managed Services App & Data Team, you'll get the opportunity to help our clients solve their complex business issues from strategy to execution and beyond.

Are you looking for a challenging job in a dynamic and innovative environment? You are about to find the perfect match!

Are you a data and IT enthusiast who also talks the language of business? Do you have a passion for solving complex data-based problems, creating, and developing reports, dashboards and troubleshooting data errors and issues?

What you will do and learn:

You help our clients in the execution of their data and analytics. Act as a key contributor to collect, clean, transform and analyze data for efficient decision making to meet business expectations. Support in the data analytics tool selection process (RfX, etc.)

Report and visualize key insights from performed analysis to be shared with key stakeholders (e.g: via PowerBI, Tableau, slides, etc.)

Working closely with different teams to ensure issues and requests are addressed timely

Identify and proactively propose actionable improvements improvements when problems and/or opportunities arise

Follow risk management and compliance procedures

Design and implement automation solutions end-to-end to streamline data processing, reporting and operational workflows, contributing to efficiency and standardization

Share and collaborate effectively with others

Take on leadership responsibilities by coordinating and guiding small teams or junior colleagues on defined work packages

Communicate confidently in a clear, concise and articulate manner – verbally and in the materials you produce

Build and maintain an internal and external network

Use creative problem-solving methods and work in an international, multidisciplinary team

Uphold the firm's code of ethics and business conduct

Seek opportunities to learn about how PwC works as a global network of firms



Who you are

A relevant university degree in IT (management) or IT technology-related programmes or Business Administration/Management, Economics, Computer and Information Science, or any other relevant university degree that showcases your capabilities

You have a strong interest in data analytics, IT tools and the opportunities they offer as well as a good understanding of how to leverage them

You have a strong ability to leverage and turn your key findings into clear and outstanding presentations

Minimum 3 years of experience in data analytics or business analysis

Proven hands-on experience developing and deploying reports, dashboards, and metrics

The ability to express yourself fluently in English and Dutch and/or French

Strong Analytical skills

Strong verbal communication skills

Strong interpersonal skills

Excellent time management skills under tight deadlines

The ability to work autonomously on assigned tasks and as part of a team

Keep upskilling yourself via training and on the job learning and actively share knowledge with junior collegues



Moreover, you have an interest, and/or initial experience in several of the following:

PowerBI and Tableau knowledge

Knowledge of Alteryx, PowerApps and Power Automate is a plus

SQL / Python knowledge is a plus

Project management & Microsoft certification is a plus (e.g. Prince II, PMI, Agile/Scrum, Azure AI, Power BI Data Analyst Associate, etc.)

Interest in new technologies like generative AI



What we offer

Valuable professional experience at the ‘best business school in the world’

Continuous learning with classroom training (both technical and soft skills) in Belgium and abroad

A workspace where everyone feels respected, valued, and free to be themselves

Inspiring and awesome colleagues

Team events such as Afterwork drinks, team-building days, and celebrations, as well as time spent on community days and charity activities

Forward-looking and very accessible leadership hosting inspirational lunches

A flexible working schedule including homeworking and the possibility to conveniently make use of various holiday schemes

An interesting salary and benefits package

And much more!



Looking for a career where you’ll really make a difference? Then we look forward to meeting you!

Title: data analyst
Description:
NEXEO is currently looking for a Data Analyst to support a major client in Brussels.

This mission is part of a data-driven transformation program aimed at strengthening reporting capabilities, improving data quality, and enabling better business decision-making across key departments.

Mission Overview

As Data Analyst, you will be responsible for transforming raw data into clear, actionable insights that support strategic and operational decisions. You will work closely with business and IT stakeholders to enhance reporting, optimize data processes, and ensure data integrity across systems.

Key Responsibilities

Extract, clean, and analyze data from multiple internal and external sources
Design, build, and maintain dashboards, reports, and KPI frameworks
Translate business requirements into data models and analytical outputs
Ensure data quality, consistency, and governance standards are respected
Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies to support business decisions
Collaborate with business and IT teams to improve data accessibility and usability
Support continuous improvement of reporting and data processes
Contribute to data modelling and optimization initiatives

Profile Required

Minimum 3–5 years of experience as a Data Analyst
Strong analytical mindset with ability to structure and interpret complex datasets
Experience in data reporting, analysis, and visualization
Degree in Data, IT, Economics, Finance, or related field

Technical Skills

Strong command of SQL
Advanced Excel skills
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools
Good understanding of data structures and databases
Knowledge of Python or R is a strong asset

Languages

English: Fluent
French and/or Dutch: Professional working knowledge

Soft Skills

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving mindset
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to translate data into business insights
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Proactive, structured, and autonomous working style
Ability to work in a fast-paced, evolving environmen

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
RedCore is an international business group that creates technological solutions for digital markets.


Our products and services cover fintech, marketing, e-commerce, customer service, communications, and regulatory technologies.


Powering Growth from the Core — this is the key mission laid under RedCore’s strong foundation. This is how we consistently stay ahead: by evolving and creating scalable solutions, while remaining committed to open and transparent communication with our partners.


Requirements:

  • Knowledge of the basic betting terms and mechanics;
  • Experience in drafting and conducting marketing activities for player retention and return;
  • Experience with client segmentation;
  • Strong English skills, with the ability to write and communicate briefs and tasks;
  • Knowledge of market and competitor analysis methods;
  • Understanding of basic approaches to A/B testing and hypothesis testing to optimize marketing activities;
  • Analytical mindset;
  • Knowledge of marketing and niche metrics: LTV, ARPU, RR, CR, GGR, NGR, etc;
  • Knowledge of esports disciplines that constantly appear in the lines (names of tournaments, teams, clubs, top players, logos, etc.).

Will be plus:

  • Understanding the odds in betting and how to calculate them;
  • Experience working with analytics tools: GA, Tableau, Voluum, Power BI, Google Data Studio.
  • Being an esports fan.

Soft Skills:

  • Attention to detail;
  • Autonomy and proactivity in work;
  • Openness and directness in communication.


Responsibilities:

  • Monthly planning and compilation of esports marketing activities;
  • Organization of promotions, raffles, and other activities to retain and return the player;
  • Creation of a communication plan with players via SMS, e-mail, messengers, push and social networks;
  • Calculation of predictions, analytics of held actions;
  • Management of expenditure planning and reporting;
  • Setting up trigger chains;
  • Communication with the team to develop a general strategy;
  • Analysis of the market and competitors;
  • Setting tasks for the employees of cross-departments.


Benefits:

  • An exciting and challenging job in a fast-growing business groups, the opportunity to be part of a multicultural team of top professionals in Development, Architecture, Management, Operations, Marketing, Legal, Finance and more
  • Great working atmosphere with passionate experts and leaders, sharing a friendly culture and a success-driven mindset is guaranteed
  • Beautiful offices in Warsaw, Limassol, Kyiv, Almaty, Yerevan — work remotely or on-site with comfort and enjoy the opportunity to build a network of connections with professionals day by day
  • Modern corporate equipment based on macOS or Windows and additional equipment are provided
  • Paid vacations, sick leave, personal events days, days off
  • ️Corporate health insurance program for your well-being
  • Referral program — enjoy cooperation with your colleagues and get the bonus
  • Educational programs: regular internal training sessions, compensation for external education, attendance of specialized global conferences
  • Rewards program for mentoring and coaching colleagues
  • Free internal English courses
  • Yoga classes to help you stay active and energized
  • In-house Travel Service
  • Multiple internal activities: online platform for employees with quests, gamification, presents and news, RedCore clubs for movie / book / pets lovers, special office days dedicated to holidays
  • Corporate events, team buildings

Title: Shopper Insights & Data Analysis Intern (AfH RTM)
Description:
Step into a world where data meets real impact.

Join Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) and bring your analytical mindset, curiosity and business sense to a company that’s always evolving.

About The Internship

The Away From Home (AfH) – RTM team is looking for an enthusiastic intern to join us in Anderlecht from June onwards for a 3 to 4-month internship (extension possible).

During this internship, you will support the team in collecting and analysing shopper and commercial data, directly contributing to the preparation of the strategic plan 2027 (ABP).

You will work on real business challenges, helping the team better understand shopper behaviour and translate insights into strategic recommendations.

What You’ll Do


Collect shopper insights across DI/DC and Cash & Carry channels (sell-out data, interviews, shop visits)
Analyse shopper behaviour in drink centers (promotions, activations, pricing, etc.) using qualitative and quantitative data
Support the development of internal data tools (e.g. spill-over WHS/BWS analysis)
Translate data into actionable insights to support strategic decision-making
Contribute to the preparation of the ABP 2027


Who You Are


Student in Economics, Business, Data or similar field
Strong analytical mindset with good Excel skills
Hands-on, proactive and able to work independently
Structured, detail-oriented and curious
Strong communication skills and positive mindset
Flexible, motivated and eager to learn
Fluent in Dutch, French and English


Why join CCEP?

We join for the brands. We stay for the people.

At CCEP, you’ll be part of a fast-paced and supportive environment where you are encouraged to take ownership, bring ideas and grow.

As an intern, you will have a direct impact on a key strategic project (ABP 2027) and gain hands-on experience in data analysis and commercial strategy.

About CCEP

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners is one of the world’s leading consumer goods companies and the largest Coca-Cola bottler globally.

In Belux, around 2,000 colleagues contribute across production, logistics, sales and headquarters, managing the full value chain locally and delivering excellence every day.

Our recruitment vision

At CCEP, everyone is welcome. We value skills, potential and motivation, regardless of background.

For GDPR reasons, please apply via our official platform.

Questions? InternshipsBelux@ccep.com

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Nexeo is currently looking for a Data Analyst (Freelance or permanent) for a market leader in the banking industry in Belgium.




Nexeo Belgium is a consultancy company that supports its clients in the banking, finance and insurance sectors in all phases of their transformation projects: from business consulting to the implementation of IT solutions. 




As a consultant you will have the opportunity to be part of Nexeo:

International environment ( Offices in France, Belgium, Luxembourg, Switzerland & Canada)
+ 450 employees
over 20% growth each year




The ideal candidate will use their passion for big data and analytics to provide insights to the business covering a range of topics. They will be responsible for conducting both recurring and ad hoc analysis for business users. 

 

Responsibilities

Understand the day-to-day issues that the bank faces
Performs cleansing of data quality issues through the analysi
Monitors the data quality based on data quality dashboards, supports redesign & upgrade
Contributes to designing, monitoring and reporting on common reference tools
Contributes to raising awareness of the importance of data quality
Will be involved in all the topics related to Customer Data
Business Analysis
Work in collaboration with other technical teams to share data
Data extraction with SAS
Industrialize the process to run define periodicity
Document the project (functional and technical part)
Contribute to the achievement of the team objectives




Qualifications

Bachelor's or Master's degree
At least 3 years of experience in Data Analysis (focus data quality & data governance)
Understand data lifecycle and data usage
Working in Agile environnement (Jira & Scrum)
Good experience working with SQL Queries & SAS
English Fluent & French or Dutch native

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Senior Data Analyst in Belgium.

This is a high-impact analytics role at the core of a fast-growing, performance-driven digital organization. You will transform complex marketing, financial, and operational data into clear insights that directly shape business strategy and decision-making. Acting as a key partner to leadership, finance, and operations teams, you will build forecasting models, reporting systems, and automated data workflows that improve visibility and drive performance. The role combines technical depth with business acumen, requiring someone who can move seamlessly between data infrastructure, analysis, and strategic storytelling. You will operate in a fast-paced, fully remote environment where ownership, precision, and scalability are essential. This position is ideal for a data professional who enjoys building systems that power real-time, high-stakes business decisions.

Accountabilities


Develop and maintain forecasting models for revenue, headcount, and business performance to support strategic planning and decision-making.
Translate historical and operational data into forward-looking insights, identifying risks, trends, and growth opportunities.
Build, maintain, and optimize dashboards using BI tools such as Power BI, Looker, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Design standardized reporting frameworks for marketing, client performance, media spend, and core business KPIs.
Automate manual reporting processes by building scalable data pipelines and integrating multiple data sources.
Manage and enhance data workflows using tools such as Supermetrics, Google Sheets, and Apps Script for seamless reporting automation.
Ensure data integrity, consistency, and quality across all reporting systems and databases.
Design and maintain structured databases to support operations, finance, and performance reporting needs.


Requirements


3-6 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or similar roles, preferably in fast-paced digital, agency, or DTC environments.
Strong proficiency in SQL, Python, or equivalent tools for data analysis, querying, and pipeline development.
Advanced expertise in Google Sheets and Excel, including complex formulas, automation, and scripting (Apps Script preferred).
Hands-on experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Looker, Tableau, or comparable platforms.
Strong understanding of marketing and performance data, including familiarity with paid media platforms like Meta, Google, and TikTok.
Experience building dashboards, reporting systems, and automated data workflows from multiple data sources.
Strong systems thinking with the ability to design scalable, reliable data infrastructure rather than one-off reports.
High attention to detail with strong problem-solving skills and data accuracy mindset.
Ability to work independently in a remote, fast-paced environment with strong ownership and accountability.
Excellent communication skills to translate data insights into clear business recommendations.


Benefits


Competitive monthly compensation in the range of $2,000-$4,000 USD.
Fully remote work setup with global team collaboration.
Opportunity to work on high-impact marketing and business intelligence systems.
Exposure to large-scale performance marketing operations and datasets.
High autonomy and ownership over analytics infrastructure and reporting systems.
Fast-paced, growth-oriented environment with strong learning opportunities.
Direct collaboration with leadership and cross-functional business teams.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst (UK/EU job market) / System Analyst (could be on CIS job market)




Department: Data Platform

Reports to: Data Engineering Lead

Employment Type: Full Time

Work Model: Fully Remote

Location: Remote EMEA




About Traffic Label

Traffic Label is a fast-growing, data-driven technology company operating in the iGaming and affiliate marketing space, building scalable products focused on performance, analytics, and automation across global markets.







What we’re building

We’re building a Customer Data Platform: one unified player profile across our group of brands. The platform should help the business:




Improve acquisition. Understand who actually converts, and why others don’
Increase conversion. Replace generic flows with journeys built for the specific player in front of us.
Segment players properly. Who’s a high-value player? Who’s at risk of leaving? Who’s a VIP?
Reactivate before churn. Spot players who are about to leave and bring them back with offers that match what they actually want.




Tech stack:

Snowflake (with Snowpipe and Cortex Analyst LLM agent).
dbt, Python.
Airflow (Astronomer), AWS (S3, Lambda), Terraform, GitHub Actions.
Omni BI on top. Reverse ETL into marketing platforms.
Medallion architecture: bronze, silver, gold.

We’re early in the build, and the person we hire helps shape the foundation.




Role Overview

You sit between the business (Product Owner, stakeholders) and the engineering team.

About 30% of your time is practical data work: validating that CDP capabilities behave as specified, exploring data before specs are written, testing hypotheses for new capabilities, building shared gold layer models that consumer teams use, and helping those teams understand what data is available. It is not business reporting or analytics built for individual brands.




The other 70% is Systems Analysis: turning what you find into specs engineers can build from, owning data quality from symptom to root cause, raising what isn’t right before it ships, and shaping the semantic layer so other business lines can find and trust the data.




Four things define the systems analysis side:

Turn business intent into specs that engineers can build from. Talk to stakeholders, figure out what they actually need, and write it down. Each spec covers source schema, target model, transformation logic, business glossary, schedule, data quality checks, consumer, and use case. Stay reachable through development. Engineers will have questions.
Find and fix data quality problems at the root, alongside the engineering function. Trace lineage, identify the cause, work with the data source owner on the fix, check downstream impact, and close the loop.
Confirm what’s delivered does what we said it would. Validate against the spec you wrote. When the data isn’t right, you raise it before it ships.
Make data usable beyond the team that asked for it. Contribute to the semantic layer, data descriptions, and shared definitions so other business lines can find what they need, understand what it means, and trust it without going through you each time.







You work on the Data Platform layer:

CDP build, identity resolution, data quality, semantic definitions, and governance. Business analytics on top of the platform, such as dashboards and performance reports for specific brands, is produced by analyst teams aligned to those brands. Your job is to make sure those teams can do theirs: trustworthy data, clear definitions, fast issue resolution.




The team

You sit within the Data Platform alongside:

A Product Owner who sets the roadmap and business priorities.
The Data Engineering function. Your manager (Data Engineering Lead) and the engineers building the platform.
Embedded QA function. Owns automated validation.
Shared DevOps across the wider organisation.




Where you’ll start

Identity resolution. Deterministic matching is already running in our MDM across five data sources. You extend coverage to the remaining sources and validate match quality at scale. The harder cases are future scope: hashed identifiers without keys, duplicates across sources, device and behaviour signals. They start when we add dedicated ML engineering capacity, with no committed timeline yet. You’ll own the spec and acceptance for that work when it begins.




Skills & Experience

SQL. Expert level. Window functions, complex joins, and root cause investigation on large datasets.
dbt. Practical experience. You can write models, write tests, and debug them.
Python. Comfortable for ad hoc analysis, scripting, and data manipulation.
Confident across the modern data stack. You investigate problems yourself instead of waiting for DE, and you can explain the root cause to the business in plain language.
Medallion or layered data architectures.
You’ve written specifications that engineers built from.
You’ve owned a data quality problem from “something looks off” to “fixed at source”.
You can talk to business stakeholders and a backend engineer in the same hour without losing either of them.
Background in iGaming, affiliate marketing, SaaS, or other high-volume digital businesses.
Experience unifying customer or user profiles across sources, sometimes called Customer 360 or Single Customer View. Angles: identity matching, semantic modelling, etc.




What We Offer

Opportunity to work on scalable, high-impact products in a growing iGaming business
Collaborative and fast-paced engineering environment
Exposure to modern technologies and architecture
Competitive salary and performance-based incentives
Flexible working environment and supportive team culture

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Our client is a leading strategy and performance agency, acting as a growth partner for brands and advertisers.




With 7 international offices and a top team of 20 Belgian experts, they combine global know-how with a personal, hands-on approach. 🌐




Their expertise?




Mass & Digital Media, Brand Content, AdTech, Search, Influencer Marketing, Data, and AI — always focused on maximizing brand performance. 🚀




Your mission




As an Account Strategist, you are the key link between the client and the expert team.

You combine strategic insight with strong people skills to ensure campaigns are not only creative but also measurably successful.




You contribute to solving business challenges, translate ambitions into concrete action plans, and coordinate multidisciplinary teams so that strategy and execution align perfectly.




What you'll do




Strategic partner

Develop integrated communication and media strategies
Translate data and insights into actionable strategies
Think omnichannel: channels reinforce each other throughout the full funnel




Account management

Act as the main point of contact for clients, building long-term partnerships
Proactively bring ideas and solutions
Monitor budgets, timing, and quality
Translate client briefings into clear project assignments




Team coordination & execution

Bring experts together and coordinate collaboration across disciplines
Manage projects and safeguard deliverables
Ensure transparent communication between client and team




Reporting & optimization

Analyze campaigns and translate results into concrete insights
Present strategies and results in a compelling way
Optimize based on performance data




Sounds like you?

2–8 years of experience in a similar role (advertising, media, or communication agency)

Strong skills in digital marketing, data analysis, and performance marketing
Strategic thinker with a hands-on mentality
Strong relationship management and communication skills (Dutch, French, English)




What we offer

Challenging full-time role in an internationally growing agency
Opportunity to work with diverse clients and exciting projects
Access to a broad expert network, with room for initiative and professional growth
Training opportunities and coaching
Office in Brussels + flexible work options (2 fixed office days)
Competitive salary + fringe benefits




Sounds like you?




Apply today through Linkedin or hello@huntr.be or feel free to send us a message!

Title: Growth Data Analyst – Digital Performance
Description:
Turn curiosity into impact. Shape the future of digital banking.

Within ING, we believe progress happens when people dare to experiment, challenge, and create.

As a Growth Data Analyst, within the Digital department, you help craft simple, personal and smart digital experiences for millions of customers — using data as your superpower.

If you’re driven by impact, love connecting dots, and thrive where ownership meets opportunity, this role is made for you.

Role Summary

You are the analytical heartbeat of our digital campaign squads.

With a mix of sharp insights, entrepreneurial thinking and a test and learn mindset, you help improve our digital funnels and campaigns — boosting conversion, activation and engagement.

You turn complexity into clarity, and insights into action and learn mindset, you help improve our digital funnels and campaigns — boosting conversion, activation and engagement.

Your Team & Environment

You’ll join the Growth department within the Digital Tribe DACI, working closely with Customer Journey Experts, product owners and marketeers.

You report to the Personalization & Martech Product Lead and are part of a passionate analytics community that continuously shares, learns, and innovates.

What You’ll Do


Partner with stakeholders to understand challenges and uncover new growth opportunities.
Analyze digital performance and campaign results, identifying levers that really move the needle.
Shape, run, and evaluate A/B tests that help ING learn faster.
Translate data into simple, actionable recommendations for squads and leadership.
Strengthen our analytics ecosystem — improving measurement, dashboards and tooling.
Share insights with a mindset of curiosity, ownership, and collaboration.


Why You’ll Love This Role


Your work has a direct, visible impact on ING’s digital commercial performance.
You operate in an environment that encourages initiative, experimentation, and continuous improvement.
You help shape how customers experience ING — every day, across Belgium.
You become a part of a culture built on helping each other succeed, living our Orange Code and embracing innovation.


Tech You’ll Use

SQL


SAS CI360
SAS eGuide
Adobe Analytics
Power BI Plus growing exposure to GenAI, advanced experimentation capabilities and innovation labs.


Your Global Playground

You collaborate with analytics experts across 40 ING markets, tapping into shared frameworks, inspiration and best practices — while keeping freedom to experiment locally.

What’s In It For You


A dynamic international digital environment with passionate data professionals.
Real autonomy and space to take things further.
A flexible work model that supports your wellbeing and creativity.
Strong rewards: bonuses, pension & insurance package, mobility budget, wellbeing programs, meal vouchers, modern digital tools and generous holidays.
Access to learning tracks, internal mobility and international contacts


What You Bring (Medior Profile)


A degree in Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Information Management, Statistics… or equivalent experience (~3+ years).
Hands on analytics experience in marketing, growth or digital environments.
Solid skills in SQL, SAS CI360, SAS eGuide, Adobe Analytics, Power BI; Python is a plus.
A curious, proactive mindset — eager to challenge, simplify and drive meaningful outcomes.


Ready to Shape Tomorrow’s Digital Experiences?

Apply via Workday on ing.be/careers — and let’s make banking better together.

Title: Data Business Analyst
Description:
Mission Overview:

Join Keystone Solutions as a Data Specialist Consultant to deliver a consultancy mission at our client. You will support the client's Data Manager and collaborate closely with the client's data and IT teams to realize data lineation (data lineage), ensure data collection, analysis, and valorization, and drive automation to support decision-making and continuous process improvement.

Key Responsibilities (Client Delivery as a Keystone Solutions Consultant):


Primary mission: realize data lineation, ensure the collection, analysis, and valorization of data with a view to automation to support decision-making and continuous process improvement.
Analysis and understanding of needs:
Gather and clarify needs from stakeholders.
Formalize business requirements in structured documents (BRD, specifications, user stories).

Data management and processing:
Analyze data sources and their quality.
Document flows, transformations, and business rules.
Contribute to the implementation of automation processes.

Technical collaboration with IT:
Work closely with the client's Data teams.
Work closely with the client's IT teams.
Validate the technical feasibility of the needs.
Follow implementation and test deliverables.

Establish links between data lineation and the description of processes.


Compliance and Standards:


Throughout the mission, comply with GDPR, ISO27001, and NIS2.



Engagement Details:


Start date to be determined by mutual agreement between the client and the selected consultant.
The designated consultant will be assigned full-time and may deliver 440 person-days spread over approximately 2 years, based on a 40-hour work week during the client's business days.
Services will be performed at least 3 days per week on-site at the client's headquarters in 1000 Brussels, potentially on non-consecutive days, unless expressly exempted by the client's Head of the IT Department.
Overtime or work outside office hours (Monday to Friday, 07:00 to 19:00) is not authorized without the client's express approval.
The consultant must consider service continuity needs and project constraints when planning leave and agree in advance with the client's Leading Official.
The consultant must comply with the client's Code of Conduct for external providers (available upon request).



Additional Information:


The mission may be renewed for a maximum duration (including the initial period) of 880 working days.



Consultancy Model at Keystone Solutions:


Consultancy nature of work: You will operate as a Keystone Solutions consultant, embedded with the client's teams, primarily on-site, to deliver tangible outcomes while benefiting from Keystone's support framework.
Dynamic projects: Engage with diverse challenges and complex data environments, with the opportunity to contribute across multiple client initiatives over time.
Turbo-charged learning and development: Access broad learning experiences and professional development support to deepen expertise in data lineage, data quality, automation, and reporting.

Title: Data Analyst H/F
Description:
À propos de nous

Nous sommes le premier industriel de la donnée en Europe. Notre métier est de créer, manager et exploiter le patrimoine data de nos clients.

Nous avons la conviction que chaque entreprise peut adopter une démarche innovante de gestion de la donnée et créer des cas d’usage disruptifs en réduisant l'impact écologique et en diminuant la dette technique.

Nous comptons plus de 900 collaborateurs en mission chez nos clients grands comptes dans tout secteur d’activité : banque, assurance, santé, énergie, e-commerce, automobile, luxe, retail, transport, agritech…

Avantages à travailler chez JEMS


Une JEMS Académie au service de votre montée en compétences (formations et certifications sur les technologies de pointe)
Un accompagnement personnalisé et un management de proximité pour vous proposer des évolutions de carrière
Une intégration dans des communautés techniques et de pratiques JEMS (encadrement par des experts, échanges sur les bonnes pratiques, favoriser l'innovation...)
Une entreprise reconnue "Great Place To Work"
Des évènements et séminaires inoubliables, des soirées d'agence conviviales


Mobilité

Une mobilité nationale et internationale pour vous accompagner dans vos projets de vie.

Diversité

Le Groupe JEMS porte fièrement sa valeur "Diversité" en se mobilisant pour l'inclusion et l'égalité des chances et en luttant contre toutes formes de discrimination.

Tous nos postes sont ouverts aux personnes en situation de handicap.

Mission

Nous recherchons un(e) Data Analyst confirmé(e) capable d'accompagner les métiers dans l'aide à la décision.

Vous Aurez La Charge De


Guider / recommander le Métier dans l’exploitation et la valorisation des multiples sources de données et identifier des leviers d’optimisation et d’amélioration des processus Business
Être l‘intermédiaire entre le Métier et la DSI
Accroître la connaissance de la clientèle d’une entreprise, conduire des études sur les bases de données, suivre les outils datamining pour analyser l’impact des actions marketing
Mener et conduire des études (coordination/pilotage)


Profil

En Tant Que Data Analyst Vous Maitrisez


MS Office
Le requêtage SQL
Des outils BI Fonction Expertise sur les données du Métier
Des solutions Analytiques (Data Science ou Data Mining / BI)
Des outils de datavisualisation
La préparation de données


Vous êtes diplômé(e) d’une école d’ingénieur ou d’un parcours universitaire technique. Vous justifiez d’au minimum 3 ans d’expérience professionnelle en tant que Data Analyst.

Une excellente maîtrise du français et de l’anglais est attendue, ainsi qu’une bonne maîtrise du néerlandais.

Référence de l'offre : 5ts8l8bhnz

Title: Data Analyst Confirmé (H/F)
Description:
Description Du Poste

Vous serez intégré(e) dans une équipe de consultants innovants et motivés, composée de Business Analysts, Data Scientists et Data Engineers spécialisés sur les problématiques Data Analytics. À ce titre, vous interviendrez de façon opérationnelle sur des missions de conseil autour des sujets de transformation Data Analytics, de retraitement et modélisation de données et de représentation visuelle de la donnée pour adresser des problématiques métiers.

Vous serez notamment en charge de :


Comprendre les problématiques métier,
Collecter et traiter les données en vue de l'analyse, incluant notamment la création de variables agrégées et complémentaires aux données brutes,
Automatiser et d’industrialiser les retraitements effectués sur les données,
Représenter les résultats dans un outil de restitution visuelle de façon ergonomique et intuitive,
Restituer les analyses au client notamment pas la création d’indicateurs pertinents pour les questions stratégiques des entreprises


PROFIL RECHERCHE

Diplômé d'une école d'ingénieurs ou équivalent (Bac +5), vous justifiez d’a minima 2 ans d’expérience réussis en tant que data analyst :


Vous possédez une bonne connaissance des outils et méthodologies de Data Analytics, incluant notamment des bases techniques (modèles de données, langage SQL, …) mais également méthodologiques,
Vous possédez une connaissance approfondie d'Excel et maitrisez les langages statistiques tels que R, Python ou SAS,
Une connaissance d'un logiciel ou langage dédié à la Data Visualization (Tableau, Qliksense ou Qlickview, Microsoft Power BI…) ainsi que des compétences en automatisation (VBA par exemple) seraient un plus.


PROCESSUS DE RECRUTEMENT


Échangez avec un ou une chargée de recrutement pour un premier contact téléphonique
Rencontrez un ou une Ingénieure d’Affaires (Business Manager) sur vos souhaits d’évolution
Approfondissez avec nos responsables et directeurs d’agence pour connaître la vision de l'agence


Selon votre profil, des tests ou entretiens techniques pourront vous être demandés. Tout au long du processus, vous pourrez échanger avec nos consultants pour en savoir + sur leur quotidien chez nous !

PACKAGE SALARIAL

Chez Extia Belgique IT, la transparence fait partie intégrante de l'expérience positive, voici un aperçu des éléments de package salarial dont peuvent profiter nos collaborateurs, sous réserve d'éligibilité

👛 Voiture de société ou budget mobilité ✨ Chèques repas & Ecocheque 🎁 Assurance groupe 🎉 Frais de représentation 📈 Plan cafétéria / Flex Income Plan 🚀 Primes d'apport d'affaire, de cooptation et de formation 🌍 20 jours de congés légaux + 12 jours de récupération

Ces informations sont communiquées à titre indicatif et seront abordées plus en détails lors du processus de recrutement.

LES PETITS PLUS

🎉 250 événements par an pour rencontrer tes collègues et des locaux feel good 📢 Des formations, conférences et une plateforme d'e-learning avec LinkedIn pour monter en compétences #greatplacetolearn 👩‍ Des Communautés Métiers et du Mentorat pour partager son expertise et en apprendre sur de nombreux sujets #greatplacetoshare 🕹️ Une communauté de e-sport : Extia Gaming ! #greatplacetoplay 🌏 Envie d’évoluer ? Grâce à notre politique de mobilité interne, explorez de nouvelles opportunités en Belgique ou dans l’un de nos 7 autres pays !

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We're looking for a consultant to join our client's team, taking on a pivotal role in implementing and maintaining high-quality data solutions for anti-money laundering (AML) processes. This position is essential for ensuring compliance, efficiency, and scalability across various operations within the organization. You will bridge the gap between data providers and data consumers, aligning technical solutions with business needs while adhering to strict data governance and quality standards.

Responsibilities

Your core responsibilities will encompass various facets of data management:


Stakeholder Alignment & Communication
Facilitate collaboration among IT, Data Hub, Compliance, and Operations to ensure consistent alignment through structured documentation, workshops, and transparent communication.
Convert complex business requirements into actionable data solutions while balancing technical feasibility with regulatory and operational needs.
Data Governance & Quality
Develop and enforce data governance frameworks, including role definitions and quality rules, in conjunction with data providers and consumers.
Proactively monitor and refine data quality issues to maintain the integrity of AML-related datasets.
Document business and technical rules for data integration to ensure compliance and traceability.
Data Analysis & Troubleshooting
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies, particularly in raw data formats.
Conduct analysis of AML Transaction Monitoring data to identify patterns and gaps affecting compliance and operational efficiency.

Role and Profile

The ideal candidate will possess a blend of technical and business expertise, including:


Technical Experience
5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, particularly within AML processes.
Hands-on proficiency in SQL and data modeling tools.
Experience with reporting and dashboard tools.
Business Expertise
Comprehensive understanding of banking data, especially AML Transaction Monitoring.
Ability to capture and manage functional and non-functional requirements for data solutions.
Knowledge of data security and IT architecture within financial services.

Soft Skills And Language Requirements

The successful candidate will demonstrate strong interpersonal skills and a proactive mindset:


Excellent communication skills in Dutch, French, and English to engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive, analytical, and problem-solving abilities, with extreme attention to detail and a focus on quality.
Adaptability and a willingness to learn, effectively linking strategic objectives to operational execution.



A Master’s degree in a quantitative discipline is preferred, though candidates with relevant analytics knowledge in other fields will also be considered. The role will involve a hybrid working model, balancing onsite and home working equally.

Contactperson & Reference


Reference #: INW25874
Jeroen Van Hoecke
jeroen.vanhoecke@i4m.be

Title: Data & Business Analyst
Description:
Who we are




Phinest is a Belgian Management Consulting firm providing tailor-made services for Bel20 and Bel20-like companies.

We help these leading companies deliver their key strategic projects across various sectors (Utilities, Pharmaceutical, Banking & Insurance, Retail, Transport, Public, Telecom).




We combine a human-sized organisation with large-scale impact. You are not just a consultant: you grow with us while making an impact for our clients.

At Phinest, you’ll find the right balance between professional growth, meaningful projects, and a collaborative environment.




Position: Business & Data Analyst




Your Mission:

Join a team at the forefront of the energy transition! As an Energy Business & Data Analyst, you will be the key link between business and IT teams, driving the design, development, and optimization.




Your Responsibilities:

Analyze business needs related to energy management and optimization.
Translate these needs into clear functional specifications for technical teams.
Collect, structure, and analyze data to identify performance drivers.
Collaborate with stakeholders (business, IT, engineering, data) to ensure project success.
Monitor performance indicators and propose continuous improvements.




Your Profile:

A unique mix of business insight and data/IT mindset.
Comfortable with data analysis, modeling, and reporting tools.
Strong ability to bridge technical and non-technical discussions.




What we offer:




Our focus is to develop a long-term collaboration with our talented consultants. Therefore we invest in our people as of day one to support them into a blossoming career within Phinest.

Title: AWS Data Analyst / Data Analyst
Description:
CANDIDATES MUST BE EU BASED




What you will do




In your day-to-day work, you will:

• Analyse and validate data to ensure accuracy and quality

• Translate business needs into clear data definitions and requirements

• Work closely with stakeholders to understand how data is used and what is

needed

• Identify and resolve data quality issues, including root cause analysis

• Support Data Engineers with data insights, clarifications, and requirements

• Contribute to solution design from a data perspective

• Document data structures, mappings, and definitions in a clear and simple

way

• Write SQL queries and support data transformations

• Make smaller adjustments in data pipelines when needed

We believe you have:

• Solid experience working with data analysis and large datasets

• Strong SQL skills

• Experience from modern data platforms, preferably AWS

• Good understanding of data modelling and Data Warehousing

• Ability to turn business needs into practical data solutions

• Experience working in agile teams

It is a plus if you also have:

• Experience with AWS tools such as S3, Glue, Athena or Redshift

• Understanding of data pipelines and data engineering practices

• Experience from banking or financial services




Who you are




You are someone who:

• Enjoys working with data and understanding how it creates value

• Is curious, structured, and hands-on

• Likes to work together with others and share knowledge

• Takes ownership and focuses on getting things done

• Is comfortable working in a changing environment

Title: Data Annotator
Description:
Job Role: Data Annotator(Luxembourgish)

Engagement: Freelancing

Time commitment : 4-5 hours per day

Rate per hour - 20 USD per hour







We are looking for Luxembourgish) native language experts to support an upcoming audio evaluation and multilingual benchmarking initiative.




This opportunity is ideal for linguists and language professionals interested in working on AI‑driven speech technologies, including Text‑to‑Speech (TTS) and Speech‑to‑Text (STT) systems.




Scope of Work

✅ Audio Evaluation

Review and assess model outputs for TTS and STT quality, including accuracy, pronunciation, and naturalness.

✅ Multilingual Benchmarking

Create and validate evaluation benchmarks aligned with local language usage, cultural context, and regional expectations.

Effort Required

Effort may increase based on project needs and quality requirements

Ideal Candidate Profile

Native fluency in the Luxembourgish language and English proficiency.
Strong understanding of grammar, pronunciation, and regional variations
Prior experience in annotation, linguistic evaluation, QA, or AI/LLM evaluation preferred
Ability to complete structured evaluation tasks within defined timelines.

Title: Data Analysis & Visualization Consultant
Description:
Data Analyst

Permanent - Consultant

FR or NL + EN




Our Digital & Data team at Orange Business is a dynamic community of expert consultants revolutionizing Customer Experience, Cloud, Data, and AI across various sectors, including retail, finance, manufacturing, life sciences, and public services.




With 30 years of expertise, we continue to drive business transformation across industries by applying the latest technologies at scale in a sustainable way.




We have a significant international presence, with 20% of our activity extending beyond Belgium, and this number continues to grow. By joining us, you will be part of a global impact on businesses around the world.




Check out some of our ongoing projects:

How can a network of insurers serving multinational companies ensure effective collaboration with partners across the globe? How can it efficiently monitor client performance and identify new opportunities? At Insurope, our role is to turn data into insight and design visual dashboards that are simple, clear, and powerful, enabling Insurope's collaborators to access meaningful insights and support their decision-making in just a few clicks.
In Brussels, as in all European cities, mobility is undergoing a major transformation: less space for cars and more space for active mobility. To better manage these changes, our work at our customer Brussels Mobility involves bringing together data from private operators and public sources to visually identify problem areas and prioritize interventions.
Our customer Sappi, a global pulp and paper company, requested our help to significantly reduce energy costs and offer capacity in energy balancing markets on a daily basis. You could join the team designing and building effective interfaces and dashboards with Looker and helping to make their data usable and consumable for decision makers.




This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys owning deliverables, working close to the business, and growing their craft in a supportive, high-standards culture.

If you want to take part in this exciting journey, please take a look at the following requirements and let’s meet if that matches your profile!




What You’ll Do:

All the responsibilities we’ll trust you with




As Data Analysis and Visualization Consultant, you’ll be at the heart of exciting projects that leverage advanced analytics and visualization tools and techniques.

Your work will help our clients optimize their business processes by giving users insights that will help steer their decision making.

Engage with clients: Interviewing and understanding your target audience to discover their needs and current challenges.
Data preparation: Sourcing and shaping the required data into a clearer and more structured format that is ready for analysis. Detecting and calculating relevant derived data insights.
Dashboard design: moving away from big lists of text and numbers to an intuitive and immersive visual design that tells a story with the data to engage the end users. Here we allow users to get insights from their data at a quick glance by using visual cues rather than sifting through a lot of information.
Collaborate and connect: Connecting business and IT to work together on enabling the setup and governance of reporting solutions.
End user support and coaching: Training & supporting end users in their self-service reporting journey.




Who you are:

What matters most for this role




Your interests & soft skills:

Interest in digital transformation and data-driven solutions
Proactive mindset and outside the box thinking to tackle challenges.
You are able to explain technical concepts to a non-technical audience.
You excel in interpersonal communication and know how to have a professional and commercial approach with customers.
You value collaboration as a way to succeed
Strong communication and presentation skills in French and/or Dutch + English




Your technical expertise:

Minimum 3 years of experience in an analytical role.
Experience with data analysis/visualization tools like Power BI, Tableau, Looker, and others.
Data Transformation and Modeling:

- You enjoy solving data transformation puzzles and shaping data.

- A good understanding of how to model data for reporting/analytical operations is essential.

Querying & data exploration skills:

- A solid knowledge of querying relational databases using SQL (SQL Server, Oracle, etc.).

- Experience with cloud-based data technologies such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, and data lake technologies such as ADLS, or similar platforms.

Business understanding & problem solving: A strong ability to think along with the business to determine what are the best insights to be found in the data.
Dashboard design:

- An affinity to understand your target audience and their information needs.

- An interest in and appreciation for good visual design and how to present data in a useful way towards the end users.




Interests and soft skills we value

What success looks like




We seek individuals who embody our values - Agile, Expert, Together, and Authentic.

You thrive on ambitious goals, collaborate openly, and make a meaningful impact with integrity.

We value professionals who combine deep expertise with a passion for learning, innovation, and exceeding expectations.

If you show initiative, accountability, and support your colleagues, you’ll flourish with us.

Excellence comes from people who lead by example and pursue personal and professional growth.




Why you should join us:

Some of the rewards of working at Orange Business




A community of experts - as Digital Natives, we put innovation at the heart of our expertise. Continuously seeking new ideas, we proudly host regular “Guild” workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions through our Data Meetups.
A place to strengthen both technical depth and professional skills (communication, ownership, stakeholder collaboration).
Exposure to real-world client contexts and business challenges where your work has visible impact.
Real opportunity to grow your autonomy and career growth possibilities.
We may work with tomorrow’s technology, but at our core we are a people-oriented organization. We value quality, feedback, continuous improvement, as much as collaboration, openness, inclusivity and well-being at work.
Hybrid way of working and modern & collaborative office in Brussels
Compelling advantages including a yearly bonus scheme, a flexible mobility with possibility for a housing budget, representation and expense allowances…




Ready to build with us?

We screen applications on an ongoing basis, with the aim of conducting conversations and interviews as we receive them.

Interested but feel that you don´t match the criteria 100%? We encourage you to apply anyway! Your perfect match might be the next job we are announcing, so why not have a head start and apply now!

Apply with your CV and (if available) links to GitHub, portfolio, or examples of work that reflect your experience.




More about Orange Business:

Orange Business is the Orange Group entity dedicated to businesses and organizations. Our unique history as a telco operator and now an IT services company enables us to support the sustainable digital transformation of enterprise businesses worldwide.

We bring the rigor of a network and connectivity expert together with the agility of a global digital solutions integrator. We combine global presence with a local approach to get the most out of digital technology, from networks and cloud to data and service platforms.

Digital & Data is the dedicated Orange Business entity for professional services in digital and data, operating as part of the Orange Business European organization. We help enterprises design, build and deliver digital and data solutions, from consulting and advisory through to large-scale implementation and managed services, combining deep technical expertise with the operational agility of a ringfenced organization. By pooling expertise across countries, we offer clients a consistent European delivery model, the ability to reuse proven capabilities at scale, and seamless integration with Orange Business' wider proposition in network, AI, trusted cloud and cybersecurity. This dedicated focus on professional services, backed by the strength and reach of Orange Business and the Orange Group, means our clients benefit from specialized digital and data expertise delivered with clear accountability, European coverage, and the assurance of a trusted long-term partner.




Check out our website for even more or connect with us on LinkedIn.
