Title: Head, Social Media
Description:
Primary Location

Belgium-Brussels

NATO Body

NATO International Staff (NATO IS)

Schedule

Full-time

Application Deadline

07-Jun-2026, 11:59:00 PM

Salary (Pay Basis)

6,467.30

Grade

NATO Grade G15-G17


SUMMARY



NATO’s Strategic Communications build trust in and support for the Alliance by strengthening public awareness and understanding of NATO’s priorities and activities. Led by the NATO Spokesperson/Director of Strategic Communications, the Office of Strategic Communications (OSC) provides communications direction and guidance to stakeholders across the NATO enterprise and coordinates strategic communications activities across all NATO civilian and military bodies. The Office carries out full-spectrum communications, analysing the information environment, designing and creating content, managing communications technology and platforms, overseeing media relations, and engaging with audiences through a wide range of channels

The Digital Communities and Content Section is the team responsible for landscape digital communication, including: campaign conception, marketing and distribution of content to engage the Alliance’s audiences in NATO missions, events, operations and activities. The Section comprises teams that manage: web, multimedia content creation, campaigns, social media, brand and corporate communications.

The role of the Head of Social Media is to develop and oversee the implementation of the social media strategy as part of OSC’s multi-channel approach to audience engagement, a critical vector for achieving the Alliance’s communications objectives.


QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCE



Essential

The incumbent must:


possess a university degree in journalism, communications, international relations or related curriculum;
have experience managing a small team;
have a minimum of 5 years of experience in the strategic coordination of multiple social media channels and platforms;
have a proven ability to communicate creatively with and engage diverse audiences;
have extensive hands-on experience with editorial production on major enterprise social platforms (e.g. X, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, Facebook);
demonstrate sound political judgment and excellent analytical and conceptual skills to translate strategy into creative, digital-first, multiplatform communication;
have expertise in evolving social media platforms and a strong understanding of their respective evolving technical requirements and use;
have experience of paid advertising and influencer marketing;
possess the following minimum levels of NATO’s official languages (English/French): V (“Advanced”) in one; I (“Beginner”) in the other.



DESIRABLE

The following would be considered an advantage:


international professional experience;
experience working in an agency environment;
an interest and/or studies in international security issues;
knowledge of NATO’s organisational structure and affiliated organisations;
experience in localisation for segmented international audiences;
experience managing a budget;
knowledge of social media monitoring tools;
experience creating such microcontent as factographs, infographs and animated gifs;
experience drawing and reviewing metrics and analytics and applying lessons learned.
MAIN ACCOUNTABILITIES



Project Management

Commission, create and post content that is novel and engaging, and in line with the campaign strategy, content strategy, channel strategy and NATO messaging. Oversee the production, posting and monitoring of social products on NATO’s flagship social media channels and platforms. Collaborate with NATO’s multimedia content creators to shape professional quality content for NATO’s owned platforms and channels Carry out and feed into assessments to continue to improve the way the Office of Strategic Communications reaches different target audiences. Administer the social media budget.

Information Management

Coordinate the social media tools used by the team for NATO’s flagship channels and platforms and commission social listening activities. Keep abreast of political developments to ensure accuracy and currency of NATO’s social media communication messages.

Stakeholder Management

Participate in a network of contacts, including internal and external information providers and key actors in the field of communications, in order to ensure the correct messaging on NATO’s flagship social media outlets. Align efforts with the different key leader social media platforms. Maintain contact with private sector partners, in particular social media platforms, to ensure coherence with best practices;

People Management

Manage social media staff to include planning workloads and staffing resources for the implementation of the social media strategy. Carry out recruitments, as necessary; manage performance and objectives setting; co-develop training plans; and actively promote team cohesion. Empower staff to carry out their roles confidently in incumbent’s absence. Promote integrity, professionalism and accountability through a sound and inclusive leadership culture.

Expertise Development

Explore and research new methods and the evolution of media tools and social networks, including in cooperation with the social media platforms. Evaluate the use and application of these methods in the framework of OSC's strategic objectives. Oversee NATO’s social media policy and the creation of guidance. Inform and advise such stakeholders as senior management, social media coordinators and content creators on the use of new methods and propose improvements to existing methods. Contribute and plan for social media activities for the enterprise platforms and channels in a coordinated way. Pilot and roll out novel ways of engaging users in NATO’s story through social media.

Knowledge Management

Continuously monitor, analyse and strive to improve the work methods, processes and related tools of the team, the section and the OSC. Assist in coordination efforts within and outside the Section and across the OSC to ensure that appropriate knowledge management systems and mechanisms are in place.

Perform any other related duty as assigned.


INTERRELATIONSHIPS



The incumbent reports to the Head of the Digital Communities and Content Section. The incumbent is also required to maintain close working relationships with other teams in the Office Strategic Communications producing social media content, as well as with other internal and external content providers and the user community.

Direct reports: 4

Indirect reports: N/a.


COMPETENCIES



The incumbent must demonstrate:


Achievement: Works to meet standards;
Analytical Thinking: Sees multiple relationships;
Clarity and Accuracy: Checks own work;
Conceptual Thinking: Applies learned concepts;
Customer Service Orientation: Takes personal responsibility for correcting problems;
Empathy: Is open to others' perspectives;
Impact and Influence: Takes multiple actions to persuade;
Initiative: Is decisive in a time-sensitive situation;
Teamwork: Solicits inputs and encourages others.
CONTRACT



Contract to be offered to the successful applicant (if non-seconded): Definite duration contract of three years; possibility of renewal for up to three years, during which the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite duration contract.

Contract clause applicable:

In accordance with the contract policy, this is a post in which turnover is desirable for political reasons in order to be able to accommodate the Organisation's need to carry out its tasks as mandated by the Nations in a changing environment, for example by maintaining the flexibility necessary to shape the Organisation's skills profile, and to ensure appropriate international diversity.

The maximum period of service foreseen in this post is 6 years. The successful applicant will be offered a 3-year definite duration contract, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years. However, according to the procedure described in the contract policy the incumbent may apply for conversion to an indefinite contract during the period of renewal and no later than one year before the end of contract.

If the successful applicant is seconded from the national administration of one of NATO’s member States, a 3-year definite duration contract will be offered, which may be renewed for a further period of up to 3 years subject also to the agreement of the national authority concerned. The maximum period of service in the post as a seconded staff member is six years.

Serving staff will be offered a contract in accordance with the NATO Civilian Personnel Régulations.

NOTE: Irrespective of previous qualifications and experience, candidates for twin-graded posts will be appointed at the lower grade. Advancement to the higher grade is not automatic, and will not normally take place during the first three years of service in the post.

Under specific circumstances, serving staff members may be appointed directly to the higher grade, and a period of three years might be reduced by up to twenty four months for external candidates. These circumstances are described in the IS directive on twin-graded posts.


USEFUL INFORMATION REGARDING APPLICATION AND RECRUITMENT PROCESS



Please note that we can only accept applications from nationals of NATO member countries. Applications must be submitted using e-recruitment system, as applicable:


For NATO civilian staff members only: please apply via the internal recruitment portal ( link );
For all other applications: www.nato.int/recruitment



Before you apply to any position, we encourage you to click here and watch our video providing 6 tips to prepare you for your application and recruitment process.

Do you have questions on the application process in the system and not sure how to proceed? Click here for a video containing the information you need to successfully submit your application on time.

When submitting your application, please ensure that your Taleo Candidate Profile is updated and that your CV is correctly uploaded in the Taleo attachments section.

More information about the recruitment process and conditions of employment, can be found at our website (http://www.nato.int/cps/en/natolive/recruit-hq-e.htm)

Appointment will be subject to receipt of a security clearance (provided by the national Authorities of the selected candidate), approval of the candidate’s medical file by the NATO Medical Adviser, verification of your study(ies) and work experience, and the successful completion of the accreditation and notification process by the relevant authorities.

NATO will not accept any phase of the recruitment and selection prepared, in whole or in part, by means of reference documents without proper quotes (plagiarism), or any tools available on internet, including but not limited to translation facilities, or generative artificial-intelligence (AI) tools. NATO reserves the right to screen applications to identify the use of such tools. All applications prepared, in whole or in part, by means of such tools will be rejected without further consideration, and NATO reserves the right to take further steps in such cases as appropriate.


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION



NATO is committed to diversity and inclusion, and strives to provide equal access to employment, advancement and retention, independent of gender, age, nationality, ethnic origin, religion or belief, cultural background, sexual orientation, and disability. NATO welcomes applications of nationals from all member Nations, and strongly encourages women to apply.

NATO is committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible working environment, where all candidates living with disabilities can fully participate in the recruitment and selection process. If you require reasonable accommodation, please inform us during your selection process.

Candidates will be required to provide documented medical evidence to support their request for accommodation.

Building Integrity is a key element of NATO’s core tasks. As an employer, NATO values commitment to the principles of integrity, transparency and accountability in accordance with international norms and practices established for the defence and related security sector. Selected candidates are expected to be role models of integrity, and to promote good governance through ongoing efforts in their work.

Due to the broad interest in NATO and the large number of potential candidates, telephone or e-mail enquiries cannot be dealt with.

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

For information about the NATO Single Salary Scale (Grading, Allowances, etc.) please visit our website . Detailed data is available under the Salary and Benefits tab.

NATO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. For more info please click here .

Applicants who are not successful in this competition may be offered an appointment to another post of a similar nature, albeit at the same or a lower grade, provided they meet the necessary requirements.

The nature of this position may require the staff member at times to be called upon to travel for work and/or to work outside normal office hours.

The organization offers several work-life policies including Teleworking and Flexible Working arrangements (Flexitime) subject to business requirements.

Please note that the International Staff at NATO Headquarters in Brussels, Belgium is a non-smoking environment.

For information about the NATO Single Salary Scale (Grading, Allowances, etc.) please visit our website . Detailed data is available under the Salary and Benefits tab.

NATO does not charge any application, processing, training, interviewing, testing or other fee in connection with the application or recruitment process. For more info please click here .

Title: Data Analysis & Visualization Consultant
Description:
Data Analyst

Permanent - Consultant

FR or NL + EN




Our Digital & Data team at Orange Business is a dynamic community of expert consultants revolutionizing Customer Experience, Cloud, Data, and AI across various sectors, including retail, finance, manufacturing, life sciences, and public services.




With 30 years of expertise, we continue to drive business transformation across industries by applying the latest technologies at scale in a sustainable way.




We have a significant international presence, with 20% of our activity extending beyond Belgium, and this number continues to grow. By joining us, you will be part of a global impact on businesses around the world.




Check out some of our ongoing projects:

How can a network of insurers serving multinational companies ensure effective collaboration with partners across the globe? How can it efficiently monitor client performance and identify new opportunities? At Insurope, our role is to turn data into insight and design visual dashboards that are simple, clear, and powerful, enabling Insurope's collaborators to access meaningful insights and support their decision-making in just a few clicks.
In Brussels, as in all European cities, mobility is undergoing a major transformation: less space for cars and more space for active mobility. To better manage these changes, our work at our customer Brussels Mobility involves bringing together data from private operators and public sources to visually identify problem areas and prioritize interventions.
Our customer Sappi, a global pulp and paper company, requested our help to significantly reduce energy costs and offer capacity in energy balancing markets on a daily basis. You could join the team designing and building effective interfaces and dashboards with Looker and helping to make their data usable and consumable for decision makers.




This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys owning deliverables, working close to the business, and growing their craft in a supportive, high-standards culture.

If you want to take part in this exciting journey, please take a look at the following requirements and let’s meet if that matches your profile!




What You’ll Do:

All the responsibilities we’ll trust you with




As Data Analysis and Visualization Consultant, you’ll be at the heart of exciting projects that leverage advanced analytics and visualization tools and techniques.

Your work will help our clients optimize their business processes by giving users insights that will help steer their decision making.

Engage with clients: Interviewing and understanding your target audience to discover their needs and current challenges.
Data preparation: Sourcing and shaping the required data into a clearer and more structured format that is ready for analysis. Detecting and calculating relevant derived data insights.
Dashboard design: moving away from big lists of text and numbers to an intuitive and immersive visual design that tells a story with the data to engage the end users. Here we allow users to get insights from their data at a quick glance by using visual cues rather than sifting through a lot of information.
Collaborate and connect: Connecting business and IT to work together on enabling the setup and governance of reporting solutions.
End user support and coaching: Training & supporting end users in their self-service reporting journey.




Who you are:

What matters most for this role




Your interests & soft skills:

Interest in digital transformation and data-driven solutions
Proactive mindset and outside the box thinking to tackle challenges.
You are able to explain technical concepts to a non-technical audience.
You excel in interpersonal communication and know how to have a professional and commercial approach with customers.
You value collaboration as a way to succeed
Strong communication and presentation skills in French and/or Dutch + English




Your technical expertise:

Minimum 3 years of experience in an analytical role.
Experience with data analysis/visualization tools like Power BI, Tableau, Looker, and others.
Data Transformation and Modeling:

- You enjoy solving data transformation puzzles and shaping data.

- A good understanding of how to model data for reporting/analytical operations is essential.

Querying & data exploration skills:

- A solid knowledge of querying relational databases using SQL (SQL Server, Oracle, etc.).

- Experience with cloud-based data technologies such as Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, and data lake technologies such as ADLS, or similar platforms.

Business understanding & problem solving: A strong ability to think along with the business to determine what are the best insights to be found in the data.
Dashboard design:

- An affinity to understand your target audience and their information needs.

- An interest in and appreciation for good visual design and how to present data in a useful way towards the end users.




Interests and soft skills we value

What success looks like




We seek individuals who embody our values - Agile, Expert, Together, and Authentic.

You thrive on ambitious goals, collaborate openly, and make a meaningful impact with integrity.

We value professionals who combine deep expertise with a passion for learning, innovation, and exceeding expectations.

If you show initiative, accountability, and support your colleagues, you’ll flourish with us.

Excellence comes from people who lead by example and pursue personal and professional growth.




Why you should join us:

Some of the rewards of working at Orange Business




A community of experts - as Digital Natives, we put innovation at the heart of our expertise. Continuously seeking new ideas, we proudly host regular “Guild” workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions through our Data Meetups.
A place to strengthen both technical depth and professional skills (communication, ownership, stakeholder collaboration).
Exposure to real-world client contexts and business challenges where your work has visible impact.
Real opportunity to grow your autonomy and career growth possibilities.
We may work with tomorrow’s technology, but at our core we are a people-oriented organization. We value quality, feedback, continuous improvement, as much as collaboration, openness, inclusivity and well-being at work.
Hybrid way of working and modern & collaborative office in Brussels
Compelling advantages including a yearly bonus scheme, a flexible mobility with possibility for a housing budget, representation and expense allowances…




Ready to build with us?

We screen applications on an ongoing basis, with the aim of conducting conversations and interviews as we receive them.

Interested but feel that you don´t match the criteria 100%? We encourage you to apply anyway! Your perfect match might be the next job we are announcing, so why not have a head start and apply now!

Apply with your CV and (if available) links to GitHub, portfolio, or examples of work that reflect your experience.




More about Orange Business:

Orange Business is the Orange Group entity dedicated to businesses and organizations. Our unique history as a telco operator and now an IT services company enables us to support the sustainable digital transformation of enterprise businesses worldwide.

We bring the rigor of a network and connectivity expert together with the agility of a global digital solutions integrator. We combine global presence with a local approach to get the most out of digital technology, from networks and cloud to data and service platforms.

Digital & Data is the dedicated Orange Business entity for professional services in digital and data, operating as part of the Orange Business European organization. We help enterprises design, build and deliver digital and data solutions, from consulting and advisory through to large-scale implementation and managed services, combining deep technical expertise with the operational agility of a ringfenced organization. By pooling expertise across countries, we offer clients a consistent European delivery model, the ability to reuse proven capabilities at scale, and seamless integration with Orange Business' wider proposition in network, AI, trusted cloud and cybersecurity. This dedicated focus on professional services, backed by the strength and reach of Orange Business and the Orange Group, means our clients benefit from specialized digital and data expertise delivered with clear accountability, European coverage, and the assurance of a trusted long-term partner.




Check out our website for even more or connect with us on LinkedIn.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
SII Belgium is currently seeking a highly skilled and experienced Data Analyst to join our team. As a Data Analyst, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and implementing big data solutions to support business objectives. You will work with large, complex data sets and be responsible for analyzing and interpreting data to identify trends and insights. You would join various teams working in the Energy, Defense, Banking or Telecom sectors.

SII is a European digital and engineering services company of 16,000 experts. SII Belgium supports large businesses in adopting new technologies and innovation management. SII values empowerment and entrepreneurship, providing a fulfilling work environment for employees. Our mission is to contribute to the design and development of future products and services for customers. SII is committed to a sustainable digital world and is recognized as a Great Workplace.  

Key Responsibilities


Develop and maintain big data solutions to support business objectives
Analyze and interpret large, complex data sets to identify trends and insights
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and implement data-driven solutions
Create and maintain data visualization and reporting tools to support decision-making
Integration between enterprise architecture and data collection.
Stay up to date with industry trends and technologies to identify new opportunities for data analysis


Qualifications


You hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in IT.
Minimum of 3 years of experience working with data technologies
Strong analytical skills and experience using statistical techniques to interpret data
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools such as Tableau and Power BI
Excellent communication and collaboration skills
Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail
You are fluent in Dutch and/or French, and fluent in English.


We Offer


A challenging and varied role in a growing company
Opportunity to work with the latest technologies
Competitive salary and benefits
Opportunity to improve your skills and knowledge through training and education
Warm and open company culture, supported by an international group


If you are passionate about Data and want to work in a dynamic and fast-paced environment, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity at SII Belgium. Let's tech together !

Title: Commercial Data Management Intern
Description:
Step into a world where data meets real impact.

Join Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) and bring your curiosity, analytical mindset, and drive to a company that’s constantly evolving.

About The Internship

The Commercial Development Team in Anderlecht is looking for a motivated intern to join our Commercial Data Management team between September 2026 and December 2026, for a duration of 3 to 6 months.

Our team creates value by building and maintaining a strong commercial universe, enabling better market understanding and supporting strategic decision-making across the business.

During the internship, you’ll support commercial data management projects, helping improve data quality, enrich our databases, and generate insights that drive commercial performance.

What You’ll Do


Identify and research new companies, markets, and segments
Grow and enrich our commercial database with qualified leads
Validate existing company and market information to ensure accuracy
Support the contact data strategy to improve data quality and usability
Collaborate with different departments on cross-functional projects
Share insights that contribute to strategic commercial decisions


Who You Are


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Applied Economics, Marketing, Business Administration, or similar
Interested in market research, data analysis, or commercial strategy
Analytical, detail-oriented, and comfortable working with data
Proactive, organized, and eager to learn
Strong communication skills and a team-oriented mindset
Basic knowledge of Excel or data tools (training will be provided)
Fluent in French and Dutch (English is a plus)


Why join CCEP?

We join for the brands. We stay for the people.

Behind every bottle are passionate and talented colleagues who make our success possible. Working at CCEP means being part of a fast-paced, performance-driven and supportive environment where you are encouraged to take ownership, bring ideas and grow.

We are proud to be recognised as a Top Employer globally and in Belux for several consecutive years, a reflection of our commitment to creating a great place to work.

What makes us a great place to work:


We love our brands
We put our people first
We grow together
We make a positive difference for our customers, colleagues and communities


As a CCEP Intern, you won’t just hear about this culture, you’ll experience it. You’ll be trusted with responsibility early, supported by experienced colleagues, and challenged to grow in an environment where collaboration and learning are key.

About Coca-Cola Europacific Partners

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) is one of the world’s leading consumer goods companies and a global leader in the beverage industry, as well as the largest Coca-Cola bottler worldwide.

Every day, our 42,000 colleagues across 31 countries make, move and sell some of the world’s most loved drinks, supporting over 2 million customers and reaching hundreds of millions of consumers.

In Belux, around 2,000 colleagues contribute across our three production sites in Ghent, Antwerp and Chaudfontaine, our logistics and distribution network, field sales teams and headquarters in Brussels. From production to in-store activation, our teams manage the full value chain locally, manufacturing a significant share of our beverages in Belgium and distributing them across the Belgian and Luxembourg markets.

Whatever our role, we are driven by the same ambition: to deliver excellence, grow responsibly and create lasting value for the communities we serve.

Our recruitment vision

At CCEP, everyone is welcome. We value skills, potential, and motivation, regardless of background, identity, or ability. We encourage candidates from all walks of life to apply and share their unique perspectives.

For GDPR reasons, please submit your application exclusively via our official platform.

If you need any adjustments during the hiring process or have questions, our team is here to help: InternshipsBelux@ccep.com

Title: Data Warehouse and Reporting Developer
Description:
COSMOTE GLOBAL SOLUTIONS NV is seeking a skilled Data Warehouse and Reporting Developer to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining data warehouse solutions and generating insightful reports to support business decision-making.

Key Responsibilities:


Design, develop, and maintain scalable data warehouse solutions
Develop and optimize ETL processes to ensure efficient data integration and transformation
Create comprehensive reports and dashboards for various business units using BI tools
Collaborate with business analysts and stakeholders to gather and analyze data requirements
Ensure data quality, consistency, and accuracy across all reporting outputs
Monitor performance and troubleshoot issues related to data warehouses and reporting systems
Document data warehouse designs, ETL processes, and reporting procedures
Stay up to date with emerging trends and technologies in data warehousing and business intelligence


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field
Minimum 13 years of proven experience as a Data Warehouse Developer, Data Engineer, or similar role
Strong knowledge of data warehouse concepts, architecture, and best practices
Hands-on experience with SQL and database technologies (e.g., PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL Server)
Proficiency in ETL tools and development
Experience with BI and reporting tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to communicate effectively with stakeholders and team members
Attention to detail and commitment to data quality and accuracy
Good command of English, both written and spoken

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Our client is a leading strategy and performance agency, acting as a growth partner for brands and advertisers.




With 7 international offices and a top team of 20 Belgian experts, they combine global know-how with a personal, hands-on approach. 🌐




Their expertise?




Mass & Digital Media, Brand Content, AdTech, Search, Influencer Marketing, Data, and AI — always focused on maximizing brand performance. 🚀




Your mission




As an Account Strategist, you are the key link between the client and the expert team.

You combine strategic insight with strong people skills to ensure campaigns are not only creative but also measurably successful.




You contribute to solving business challenges, translate ambitions into concrete action plans, and coordinate multidisciplinary teams so that strategy and execution align perfectly.




What you'll do




Strategic partner

Develop integrated communication and media strategies
Translate data and insights into actionable strategies
Think omnichannel: channels reinforce each other throughout the full funnel




Account management

Act as the main point of contact for clients, building long-term partnerships
Proactively bring ideas and solutions
Monitor budgets, timing, and quality
Translate client briefings into clear project assignments




Team coordination & execution

Bring experts together and coordinate collaboration across disciplines
Manage projects and safeguard deliverables
Ensure transparent communication between client and team




Reporting & optimization

Analyze campaigns and translate results into concrete insights
Present strategies and results in a compelling way
Optimize based on performance data




Sounds like you?

2–8 years of experience in a similar role (advertising, media, or communication agency)
Solid understanding of the media and communication landscape (online & offline)
Strong skills in digital marketing, data analysis, and performance marketing
Strategic thinker with a hands-on mentality
Strong relationship management and communication skills (Dutch, French, English)




What we offer

Challenging full-time role in an internationally growing agency
Opportunity to work with diverse clients and exciting projects
Access to a broad expert network, with room for initiative and professional growth
Training opportunities and coaching
Office in Brussels + flexible work options (2 fixed office days)
Competitive salary + fringe benefits




Sounds like you?




Apply today through Linkedin or hello@huntr.be or feel free to send us a message!

Title: Consultant Data Engineer
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.

Job Description

The Data Science team is young, diverse and growing. As we are still relatively small you will be in the driver seat to shape its future. We have a flat structure and value the entrepreneurial spirit of our members: initiative is highly encouraged. We are also striving for well-being, diversity and knowledge-sharing inside the team, so that we all grow.

As a Data engineer consultant you will be working on various client projects as well as on building our own Sia AI solutions, together with our French Data Engineering team:


Cloud Services at GCP, AWS and Azure: architecture choices, use of storage & compute services, cost monitoring and optimization, role management, Infrastructure as Code
Docker and Kubernetes: containerization & orchestration of applications, management of K8s clusters adapted to Data Science workloads
Python programming: development of server-side tools (bulk data processing, REST API server, authentication, ...)
CI/CD: managing the integration and ongoing deployment of our applications and internal platforms, supporting the deployment of Data Science projects at our clients' sites
Data pipelines: development of ETL scripts in Big Data environments
Infrastructures & Services adapted to Machine Learning: technology watch and implementation of solutions useful to Data Scientists in the learning and provision of their ML models.


Qualifications


You hold a Master’s Degree with a strong quantitative component (e.g. Econometrics, Physics, Mathematics, Statistics, Quantitative Finance)
You have 1-3 years of relevant experience in Data, DevOps, Cloud or Software Engineering.
You have a good level in Python and have a solid foundation in other languages. You are familiar with at least one SQL DBMS (ideally PostgreSQL) and ideally noSQL (ideally MongoDB).
Ideally, you are already familiar with at least one cloud services platform (GCP, AWS, Azure) with experience mixing different services within the same project. If not, you have a strong appetite to learn more about these topics.
You are comfortable using Docker, automating CI/CD pipelines and have a strong appetite for gaining competence in Kubernetes if you do not yet have experience with this solution.
You will be able to work in a DevOps culture, taking responsibility for the code lifecycle, from development to deployment and maintenance of applications (mastery of the Gitlab-CE ecosystem is a plus). You are also familiar with Infrastructure as Code concepts and tools (ideally Terraform).
You are sensitive to security issues and have experience in implementing application protection solutions (certificate management, HTTPS security, OAuth2 authentication, credential management)
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
You have excellent interpersonal and team working skills
Excellent English, Dutch & French verbal and written skills are required.


Additional Information

Sia is certified "Great Place to Work". Come and join us to take part in this great company.

Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Realtime Analyst
Description:
About The Job

We are looking for a proactive and highly organized Real-Time Analyst who thrives in a fast-paced, client-focused environment. If you are passionate about optimizing service performance, monitoring live traffic, and ensuring all client contacts are handled efficiently, this is the role for you.

As a Real-Time Analyst, you will be the owner of intraday traffic, ensuring all calls, chats, and digital contacts are answered while meeting KPIs, service levels, and sales targets. You will collaborate closely with Planning, Front Office Teams, and Performance to anticipate client behavior, optimize resources, and implement real-time solutions.

This role requires quick thinking, strong analytical skills, and excellent communication, as you will continuously engage with multiple teams to align priorities, adapt staffing, and maintain operational excellence.

Job responsibilities

Real-Time Traffic & Performance Ownership


Monitor live volumes across all channels (calls, chats, digital) to ensure coverage and service levels.
Track adherence, activity codes, shrinkage, and Average Handling Time, addressing anomalies promptly.
Take ownership of KPI achievement and team performance, ensuring all client contacts are answered efficiently.
Communicate real-time updates to the team and leadership, providing actionable feedback and guidance.
Implement immediate solutions to mitigate negative impacts on service levels and sales performance.


Analysis, Forecasting & Optimization


Perform daily intraday reforecasting to align staffing with real-time traffic.
Analyze volumes, client behavior, and commercial channel trends to anticipate demand.
Identify opportunities to optimize workflows, automate repetitive tasks, and improve efficiency.
Collaborate with Planning to adjust schedules, resources, and offline activities in real time.


Resource Allocation & Planning Collaboration


Allocate resources effectively based on live traffic, performance trends, and upcoming launches.
Anticipate staff behavior and availability to maintain service levels.
Work closely with Planning to optimize schedules, considering recruitment, training, and onboarding periods.
Schedule offline activities (training, meetings) to minimize operational disruption.


Communication & Stakeholder Management


Maintain constant communication with Front Office teams, ensuring alignment and coverage.
Act as the point of contact for intraday traffic questions and decisions.
Provide visibility on challenges, priorities, and opportunities to Performance and CS management.
Collaborate across teams to ensure accurate and timely resource deployment.


Commercial & Client-Centric Focus


Understand commercial priorities, launches, and sales targets to anticipate demand.
Adapt intraday staffing to maximize client satisfaction, sales, and retention.
Monitor client behavior patterns to ensure proactive service and efficient handling.


Tools, Systems & Training


Maintain strong knowledge of WFM, CRM, and front-office systems.
Ensure accurate data entry, reporting, and adherence to operational protocols.


Profile


Relevant experience in a related role or professional environment
Consistent performance and reliability within previous roles
Strong command of English
Additional languages are a plus, reflecting our international footprint
Highly proficient in Excel
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
Ability to build strong working relationships across departments


Additional information

Discover the vibrant work culture at LVSE! Explore #LouisVuittonCSCEurope on LinkedIn to see how we thrive 🌟

Louis Vuitton Services Europe is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We celebrate the unique perspectives and talents of our team members and welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds.

At LVSE, we are committed to integrating sustainability into every aspect of our operations, ensuring that our practices reflect our dedication to environmental responsibility and contribute to a more sustainable future.

If you require any support or access accommodations, we encourage you to advise us so that we can support you through your recruitment journey.

Title: Application Security Engineer ( m/w/d)
Description:
Everience is an international consulting group delivering AI-augmented digital services and placing people at the heart of the AI revolution.

With a presence in Europe, Africa, Asia and America, Everience offers its 4,000-strong workforce the most demanding and stimulating environment in which to transform and develop their skills, learning about new AI-based roles and building their future employability.

Through its Symbiotic Academy the group offers a unique hub for training, practical application and exchange where everyone can experiment, learn, and progress in the fields of artificial intelligence and data.

In accordance with its core purpose of orchestrating the symbiotic relationship between humans and AI in the workplace, Everience is making the augmented employee the driving force of a “symbiotic age”, where AI enhances talents and opens up new career opportunities.

Job Description

In a fast evolving environment of distributed application development (Java / mobile), we are looking for a Medior Application Security Engineer to join a dedicated Application Security & Vulnerability Management team.

This role is key in strengthening application security practices across development teams and supporting secure software delivery.

Key Responsibilities:

Application Security & Developer Support:


Manage requests from development teams regarding application security tools
Perform code reviews using SAST tools
Analyze third-party libraries using SCA tools
Support development teams in adopting secure coding and SecDevOps practices
Deliver training sessions on secure development (e.g., common vulnerabilities)
Coach teams on security tools usage


Tools & Infrastructure Management:


Maintain and manage the infrastructure for automated code analysis
Ensure compliance with security policies and standards
Monitor and follow up on security evaluations
Keep secure development guidelines up to date
Review and track vulnerabilities and defects


Reporting & Continuous Improvement:


Build and maintain reports on application vulnerabilities
Contribute to improving security processes and detection mechanisms
Act as a SME in application security initiatives


Advisory Role:


Support developers in resolving security risks and incidents
Provide recommendations and practical solutions to improve application security


Qualifications


You have experience in the development of applications and are up to date with the current evolutions in the domain of application development
You have strong interest in application security and your ambition is to be become an expert in this domain in the next 3-5 years.
You have a very good understanding of Software development lifecycle in an Agile environment and you understand DevOps
You have a good understanding of software development lifecycle and the security checks to be applied at different stages
You are up-to-date on the recent developments in the area of software development: from programming languages and technologies to the standard tools and platforms. (Jenkins, Gitlab, Maven, Docker…)
You see application security as the field of in which you want to grow your expertise.
You have an interest in penetration testing and first experience in it
You are already knowledgeable with respect to network security.
You understand the difficulties related to mobile application development & testing.
You are fluent in French, English & Dutch


Additional Information

Experience in high security environments (e.g., regulated industries) is a big plus.

All our positions are open to both women and men and are, of course, open to people with disabilities.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Business Analyst (Financial Services) - No Freelancers




Position Overview

DigiTribe is looking for a Business Analyst who thrives in an Agile environment and plays a key role in delivering value to our clients. Working closely with business and technical teams, you will help define, refine, and prioritize requirements to ensure the delivery of simple, impactful, and strategically aligned solutions.







Key Responsibilities

Collaborate daily with stakeholders to understand their needs and co‑create value‑driven solutions.
Translate business needs into clear, testable, and actionable requirements.
Facilitate communication between business and technical teams to ensure shared understanding.
Contribute to backlog prioritization with a focus on value, impact, and simplicity.
Identify risks, dependencies, and opportunities for continuous improvement.
Support the team in delivering incremental features, favouring adaptability over rigidity.







Skills & Qualifications




Technical Skills

Proven experience as a Business Analyst, Functional Analyst, or similar role.
Familiarity with Business Process Modelling and tools (BPMN, Visio, Draw.io, Adonis, amongst others)
Bachelor or Master degree or equivalent by experience.
Fluent in English and French or Dutch
Experience with Agile tools (Jira, Azure DevOps…) or project management skills are a plus.
Experience with Cards & Payements is a strong asset




Soft Skills & Agile Mindset




Excellent analytical, communication, and synthesis skills.
Strong collaboration, active listening, and facilitation abilities.
Comfortable working in a dynamic and evolving environment.







Our Offer

We provide a stimulating, people‑focused and expertise‑driven environment:

A competitive salary with performance‑based bonuses.
A company car with Fuel/Electric Card
All necessary tools for your mission: Laptop, Phone, Phone Subscription.
Comprehensive social security and health benefits in Belgium.
Meal Vouchers, Eco cheques.
Hospitalization Insurance & Group Insurance (Retirement)
Opportunities for professional training and certifications.
Clear Career plan and growth opportunities created together with Manager.
Exciting assignments with clients engaged in innovative IT projects.







About DigiTribe

DigiTribe is a consulting company specialized in Cyber Security, Cloud, Architecture, Digital Transformation and Data & AI. We develop our services in collaboration with our expert community to bring innovation, digital practices, and meaningful impact to large organizations undergoing transformation.

Our added value goes far beyond providing consultants: we co‑create with our community, run workshops, produce content, and bring a modern vision of consulting.

We strongly believe in the power of a connected super‑community, an organic network fostering many‑to‑many relationships to seize opportunities and solve challenges that traditional approaches cannot address.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Job Title

Business Intelligence Analyst

Job Description Summary

Job Description

Position Title: Business Intelligence Analyst

Department: Data Analytics & Business Intelligence EMEA

Reports To: Business Intelligence Lead Netherlands

Location: Brussels, Belgium

Hybrid Working: Mainly office-based; option to work from home 1–2 days per week after onboarding

Cushman & Wakefield (NYSE: CWK) is a leading global commercial real estate services firm for property owners and occupiers with approximately 52,000 employees in nearly 400 offices and 60 countries. In 2024, the firm reported revenue of $9.4 billion across its core service lines of Services, Leasing, Capital markets, and Valuation and other. Built around the belief that Better never settles, the firm receives numerous industry and business accolades for its award-winning culture. For additional information, visit www.cushmanwakefield.com.

Role Purpose

As a Business Intelligence Analyst based in Brussels, you will play a key role in shaping Belgian and Luxembourg analytics offerings. Collaborating closely with local teams and leadership, you will leverage data to drive insights, enhance client value and propel our business operations forward.

Your role is nested within the larger EMEA Analytics team, ensuring synergy with a wide array of talent including data scientists and automation specialists. You will report directly to the BI Lead of the Netherlands.

You will specialise in analysing and interpreting data, providing actionable insights to inform strategic decisions for both our clients and internal operations. Your exceptional analytical skills, combined with a deep understanding of BI tools, will enable you to visualise and communicate complex data effectively. This high-visibility role is not just about analysing numbers, it's about being at the heart of innovation and playing a key role in driving significant, positive change across our organisation.

Key Responsibilities


Improve internal data processes, storage and reporting by launching BI projects and tools.
Simplify and visualize complex data through reports and dashboards.
Collaborate with the wider EMEA Analytics team to ensure alignment and best outcomes, and facilitate communication between the EMEA team and local team.
Identify successful BI initiatives from the EMEA team and scale them to Belgium and Luxembourg.
Work closely with local teams and clients, translating their needs and feedback into actionable analytics projects.
Ensure transparency and clarity in all BI projects and deliverables.
Convert strategic business inquiries into actionable insights by actively seeking out and defining necessary analytics resources and requirements.
Support business objectives by tracking KPIs and enabling smarter, data-driven decisions.


Knowledge & Experience


Fluent in English, with Dutch or French as a plus.
Bachelor’s degree in data science, computer sciences, economics or any related field.
Excellent skills and experience in data visualization tools ( e.g. Power BI or Tableau).
Good knowledge of databases, SQL and data modelling; experience with Python is a plus.
Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) and collaboration tools (SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, able to translate complex data into actionable insights.
Familiarity with GIS tools (e.g. ArcGIS, QGIS) and spatial data analysis is a plus.
Ability to work independently, take ownership and deliver projects from start to finish.
Committed, able to work to deadlines, prioritise and juggle between tasks.
Strong communication skills to present findings and to keep communication lines open between the different teams.


We foster a culture of inclusion that embraces the unique strengths, perspectives, and experiences of all our employees. We firmly believe that our diversity enhances our team's capabilities, leading to improved decision-making, innovation, and business outcomes. If you have any reservations about applying, please don't hesitate to reach out to your local recruiter for additional information

Hybrid Working: Mainly office-based; option to work from home 1–2 days per week after onboarding

Cushman & Wakefield (NYSE: CWK) is a leading global commercial real estate services firm for property owners and occupiers with approximately 52,000 employees in nearly 400 offices and 60 countries. In 2024, the firm reported revenue of $9.4 billion across its core service lines of Services, Leasing, Capital markets, and Valuation and other. Built around the belief that Better never settles, the firm receives numerous industry and business accolades for its award-winning culture. For additional information, visit www.cushmanwakefield.com.

Role Purpose

As a Business Intelligence Analyst based in Brussels, you will play a key role in shaping Belgian and Luxembourg analytics offerings. Collaborating closely with local teams and leadership, you will leverage data to drive insights, enhance client value and propel our business operations forward.

Your role is nested within the larger EMEA Analytics team, ensuring synergy with a wide array of talent including data scientists and automation specialists. You will report directly to the BI Lead of the Netherlands.

You will specialise in analysing and interpreting data, providing actionable insights to inform strategic decisions for both our clients and internal operations. Your exceptional analytical skills, combined with a deep understanding of BI tools, will enable you to visualise and communicate complex data effectively. This high-visibility role is not just about analysing numbers, it's about being at the heart of innovation and playing a key role in driving significant, positive change across our organisation.

Key Responsibilities

  • Improve internal data processes, storage and reporting by launching BI projects and tools.
  • Simplify and visualize complex data through reports and dashboards.
  • Collaborate with the wider EMEA Analytics team to ensure alignment and best outcomes, and facilitate communication between the EMEA team and local team.
  • Identify successful BI initiatives from the EMEA team and scale them to Belgium and Luxembourg.
  • Work closely with local teams and clients, translating their needs and feedback into actionable analytics projects.
  • Ensure transparency and clarity in all BI projects and deliverables.
  • Convert strategic business inquiries into actionable insights by actively seeking out and defining necessary analytics resources and requirements.
  • Support business objectives by tracking KPIs and enabling smarter, data-driven decisions.

Knowledge & Experience

  • Fluent in English, with Dutch or French as a plus.
  • Bachelor’s degree in data science, computer sciences, economics or any related field.
  • Excellent skills and experience in data visualization tools ( e.g. Power BI or Tableau).
  • Good knowledge of databases, SQL and data modelling; experience with Python is a plus.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) and collaboration tools (SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams).
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, able to translate complex data into actionable insights.
  • Familiarity with GIS tools (e.g. ArcGIS, QGIS) and spatial data analysis is a plus.
  • Ability to work independently, take ownership and deliver projects from start to finish.
  • Committed, able to work to deadlines, prioritise and juggle between tasks.
  • Strong communication skills to present findings and to keep communication lines open between the different teams.

We foster a culture of inclusion that embraces the unique strengths, perspectives, and experiences of all our employees. We firmly believe that our diversity enhances our team's capabilities, leading to improved decision-making, innovation, and business outcomes. If you have any reservations about applying, please don't hesitate to reach out to your local recruiter for additional information

INCO: “Cushman & Wakefield”

Title: IT Functional Analyst- Non-Life Insurance
Description:
For one of our clients active in the insurance sector, we are looking for an experienced IT Functional Analyst Non-Life Insurance to join a dynamic and innovative IT environment based in Brussels.




Your Role:

As an IT Functional Analyst, you will act as the bridge between Business and IT teams by translating business needs into functional and technical solutions.

You will contribute to the delivery of strategic projects within a complex and fast-paced environment.




Main Responsibilities:

• Analyze and translate business requirements into functional specifications

• Collaborate closely with Business stakeholders and technical teams

• Support the conversion of functional analysis into technical design

• Prepare and execute functional integration test cases

• Coordinate implementation activities with developers and designers

• Ensure delivered solutions meet business expectations

• Identify and analyze corrective actions when needed

• Report project progress to managers and project leaders




Required Skills & Experience:

• 2 to 5 years of experience as an IT Functional Analyst

• Experience working in large and complex environments

• Strong analytical and communication skills

• Ability to work autonomously and collaboratively

• Experience with multi-layer applications

• Knowledge of SQL is a plus

• Experience in the insurance sector is highly appreciated

• Familiarity with methodologies such as Prince2 or TMAP is an advantage




Profile:

• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering or equivalent experience

• Result-oriented with strong attention to detail

• Proactive, structured and client-focused mindset

• Fluent in English and either French or Dutch

You are looking for a challenging opportunity within a collaborative and business-oriented IT environment? Feel free to reach out.

Title: Functional Analyst
Description:
Publication date 1779141600000

Company DYNAFIN CONSULTING

Contract type CDI

Location Bruxelles - Belgique

Apply

Functional Analyst

Présentation de l'entreprise

DynaFin Consulting is a management consulting company delivering services to the Financial Services Industry.

We provide high value solutions through a team of > 160 committed consultants active in Belgium, France and Luxembourg.

In July 2017, we joined the Alan Allman Associates ecosystem, adding an important international dimension to our ambitions.

We specialize our services in several business domains: Invest – Payments – Credits – Insurance – Data - Risk & Compliance.

Since its creation in 2010, DynaFin has become a consulting reference in the Financial Services landscape, with > 80 Financial Institutions that trust our expertise.

We provide unique and pragmatic solutions to address market challenges, bundled in 5 DynaFin core expertises:


 Business Model Innovation
 Enterprise Operating Performance
 Regulatory Compliance
 Risk Management
 Technology


Our strenght is our TEAM !

We invest in continuous development of our talents and provide with tailored career tracks.

Rôles & Responsabilités

We are looking for a Functional Analyst to join our team and support the analysis and implementation of financial applications within the banking sector. In this role, you will act as the bridge between business stakeholders and IT teams, translating business needs into clear functional specifications and ensuring that solutions are properly designed, documented and aligned with operational and regulatory requirements.

Responsibilities


Analyze and document functional requirements based on business needs
Define and validate functional flows, use cases, and specifications
Collaborate with business analysts, developers, and testers throughout the delivery cycle
Create and maintain documentation including software requirements, data models, manuals, and test plans
Define and execute test plans and support UAT coordination
Ensure traceability of requirements and alignment with architecture and compliance standards
Support production incidents and perform root cause analysis
Contribute to process optimization and data quality remediation initiatives
Participate in Agile ceremonies and contribute to sprint planning and reviews


Profil


Minimum 4 years of experience as a Functional Analyst
Proven experience in the banking sector is mandatory
Language skills: Fluency in two of the following – English, French, Dutch
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to translate complex business needs into clear functional designs
Familiarity with Agile methodologies and collaborative delivery models
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Able to work independently and within cross-functional teams

Title: Business intelligence
Description:
Business Intelligence Specialist – Marketing Team

🏢 Starting Finance

📍 Roma - EUR: Via Francesco Patrizio da Cherso 30

🕒 Full-time: tre giorni a settimana in presenza e due giorni di smart working




Starting Finance è la community di educazione finanziaria più grande in Italia. Ogni giorno aiutiamo centinaia di migliaia di giovani a comprendere economia, mercati e attualità finanziaria attraverso contenuti digitali, eventi, formazione e prodotti innovativi.




Per supportare la crescita del gruppo, siamo alla ricerca di una figura di Business Intelligence Specialist da inserire all’interno del team Marketing.

La persona lavorerà a diretto riporto della Head of Marketing e avrà un ruolo strategico e trasversale nello sviluppo di strumenti analitici, dashboard e sistemi di reporting a supporto delle attività dei diversi team del gruppo.




Cerchiamo una persona capace di trasformare dati complessi in insight concreti e strumenti operativi che aiutino il business a prendere decisioni più rapide ed efficaci.




Di cosa ti occuperai:

Sviluppare dashboard, report e tool analitici a supporto delle attività marketing e business
Creare sistemi di monitoraggio KPI per campagne, contenuti, funnel e performance operative
Analizzare dati provenienti da diverse fonti (CRM, ADV platform, analytics, database, social media, ecc.)
Collaborare con team cross-funzionali per supportare decisioni strategiche data-driven
Automatizzare processi di reporting e raccolta dati




Chi cerchiamo

Esperienza di almeno 2 anni in ruoli BI, Data Analyst o Marketing Analytics
Ottima conoscenza di SQL
Esperienza con strumenti di data visualization (Power BI, Looker Studio, Tableau o simili)
Familiarità con Google Analytics, Meta Ads, Google Ads e CRM
Forte capacità analitica e approccio orientato al problem solving
Attitudine proattiva e capacità di lavorare su progetti trasversali
Ottime capacità comunicative e di collaborazione




Nice to have

Conoscenza di Python o strumenti di automazione dati
Esperienza con data warehouse e architetture cloud
Esperienza in startup, media company o aziende digitali
Interesse per economia, finanza e innovazione digitale




Cosa offriamo

Un ambiente giovane, dinamico e in forte crescita
Impatto diretto sulle decisioni strategiche aziendali
Possibilità di lavorare su progetti trasversali a tutto il gruppo
Team ambizioso e altamente collaborativo
Opportunità di crescita professionale e formazione continua




Se ami i dati, la tecnologia e vuoi costruire strumenti che aiutino un’intera organizzazione a crescere in modo data-driven, inviaci la tua candidatura.

Title: HR Data Analyst
Description:
Siamo il Gruppo Lottomatica, leader nel settore del gioco regolamentato in Italia e uno dei maggiori operatori europei, in tutte le aree di attività: scommesse sportive, gioco online e macchine da intrattenimento. Offriamo ai nostri clienti esperienze di gioco sicure e di qualità, attraverso tutti i canali, ponendo la responsabilità e la sostenibilità al centro del business.

Essere in Lottomatica è molto più che far parte di un'azienda: significa contribuire a un progetto imprenditoriale grande e responsabile




Siamo alla ricerca di un profilo che entrerà a far parte del Dipartimento HR come HR Data Analyst.

Principali attività:




Raccolta, aggiornamento e analisi dei dati HR provenienti dai sistemi aziendali
Preparazione di report periodici e dashboard a supporto del management
Monitoraggio dei principali indicatori HR
Supporto nell’elaborazione di analisi comparative e benchmark interni/esterni
Verifica della qualità e coerenza dei dati presenti nei database HR
Collaborazione alla predisposizione di presentazioni e documentazione a supporto dei processi HR
Partecipazione a progetti trasversali di miglioramento dei processi HR




Requisiti:

Laurea in Economia, Ingegneria, Statistica
Ottima conoscenza di Excel (database, tabelle pivot, formule avanzate)
Capacità analitica e attenzione al dettaglio
Attitudine al lavoro in team e gestione delle scadenze
Proattività ed orientamento al risultato.




Cosa offriamo:

Contratto a tempo indeterminato e opportunità di lavoro ibrido;
Laptop aziendale;
Ticket restaurant;
Formazione continua su hard skills e soft skills;
Assicurazione sanitaria integrativa;
Stipendio annuo lordo commisurato all'esperienza fino a 38k.




---




Lottomatica attua una politica che garantisce pari opportunità e promuove diversità e inclusione, contro ogni forma di discriminazione. In lottomatica crediamo che ogni persona sia unica e consideriamo la diversity come una fonte di arricchimento personale e organizzativo. Ci impegniamo ogni giorno affinché il nostro sia un ambiente aperto, sicuro e inclusivo, dove ogni persona possa essere pienamente se stessa indipendentemente da genere, religione, sesso, età, etnia, nazionalità, disabilità, stato civile o orientamento sessuale




We are Lottomatica Group, the leader in the regulated gaming sector in Italy and one of the largest operators in Europe across all business areas: sports betting, online gaming, and gaming machines. We offer our customers safe, high-quality gaming experiences through all channels, placing responsibility and sustainability at the core of our business.

Being part of Lottomatica means much more than simply working for a company: it means contributing to a large and responsible entrepreneurial project.

We are looking for a candidate to join the HR Department as an HR Data Analyst.




Main Responsibilities




Collecting, updating, and analyzing HR data from company systems
Preparing periodic reports and dashboards to support management
Monitoring key HR indicators (KPIs)
Supporting comparative analyses and internal/external benchmarking
Ensuring the quality and consistency of data in HR databases
Assisting in the preparation of presentations and documentation supporting HR processes
Participating in cross-functional projects aimed at improving HR processes




Requirements




Degree in Economics, Engineering, or Statistics
Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Excel (databases, pivot tables, advanced formulas)
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Teamwork skills and ability to manage deadlines
Proactive attitude and results-oriented mindset




What We Offer

Permanent employment contract and hybrid working opportunities
Company laptop
Meal vouchers
Continuous training on both hard and soft skills
Supplemental health insurance
Gross annual salary commensurate with experience, up to €38,000




Lottomatica is committed to an equal opportunity policy that promotes diversity and inclusion and opposes all forms of discrimination. At Lottomatica, we believe that every person is unique, and we view diversity as a source of both personal and organizational enrichment. We are committed every day to fostering an open, safe, and inclusive environment where everyone can fully be themselves, regardless of gender, religion, sex, age, ethnicity, nationality, disability, marital status, or sexual orientation.

Adecco Italia S.p.A., per importante azienda cliente, leader nel settore della mobilità ricerca:




Junior Data Analyst_Automotive




La risorsa, all’interno di un contesto organizzativo internazionale e dinamico, sarà inserita nel Dipartimento Fleet Operations e supporterà le seguenti attività:




monitoraggio e analisi del fermo tecnico, degli interventi di assistenza stradale e courtesy car
monitoraggio performance network di fornitori
analisi e monitoraggio dei KPI interni, elaborando report dettagliati con obiettivo di garantire la massima efficienza operativa, la continuità ed il miglioramento del servizio

Ottima padronanza di Excel, con capacità di analisi dati e gestione KPI. Preferenziale la conoscenza di Power BI.
Forte attitudine al problem-solving, capacità di lavorare in un ambiente dinamico e innovativo, orientamento al raggiungimento degli obiettivi.
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese (B2/C1).




Sede di lavoro: Roma.

E’ previsto un contratto iniziale di somministrazione a tempo determinato. Ral 35k.

  • Forte attitudine al problem-solving, capacità di lavorare in un ambiente dinamico e innovativo, orientamento al raggiungimento degli obiettivi.
  • Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese (B2/C1).


Sede di lavoro: Roma.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
NEVERHACK ( https://neverhack.com/it ) è un gruppo francese specializzato in cybersecurity.

Fondato nel 2021 e con sedi in 10 paesi, il gruppo conta oggi più di 1200 dipendenti in tutto il mondo.

La nostra ambizione è quella di espanderci a livello internazionale per costruire un mondo digitale sicuro per tutti.




Cerchiamo un* Data Analyst con una solida padronanza dello strumento per supportare l’analisi e la visualizzazione dei dati aziendali. Il profilo ideale è in grado di tradurre esigenze di business in report e dashboard efficaci, contribuendo alla diffusione della cultura del dato all'interno dell'organizzazione.




Responsabilità principali




Supporto tecnico per le attività di datalab
Analisi dei casi d'uso aziendali per identificare requisiti e miglioramenti per i progetti dati
Miglioramento della qualità dei dati (pulizia, coerenza, integrità, utilizzo, atomicità)
Progettazione, implementazione e ottimizzazione di progetti dati
Modellazione e mappatura dei dati
Attività di supporto e risoluzione dei problemi relativi ai progetti dati
Produzione di documentazione tecnica
Documentazione di reporting
Data lineage, catalogazione e governance
Supporto agli utenti




Requisiti




Competenze in Python, SQL e NoSQL
Competenze in Dataiku, PowerBI, Nextcloud, PostgreSQL, Opensearch, Opensearch Dashboard
Conoscenza di processi e strumenti ETL
Conoscenza di modellazione e mappatura dei dati
Conoscenza dell'analisi dei casi d'uso dei dati
Esperienza con dati di processi aziendali industriali




Cosa offriamo




Inserimento in un team giovane e motivato, con percorsi strutturati di formazione e crescita.
Ambiente stimolante in ambito cybersecurity e IT, con possibilità di sviluppo su progetti complessi.
Modalità di lavoro on-site 5 su 5
CCNL Metalmeccanico, ticket restaurant 8 €, welfare, RAL 30.000 – 40.000 € commisurata all'esperienza










Sede di lavoro: Roma, zona Termini

Il processo di selezione prevede colloquio HR e 2 colloqui di carattere tecnico.

Title: Candidatura Spontanea Neolaureati
Description:
CRESCI CON NOI: CREA UN FUTURO DIGITALE E SOSTENIBILE!

Se hai appena concluso, o stai concludendo, i tuoi studi e sei alla ricerca di un'opportunità per far emergere il tuo potenziale, allora sei nell’Azienda giusta. Sarai affiancato dai migliori professionisti per continuare ad apprendere. Il tuo successo è il nostro obiettivo comune!

In Engineering cerchiamo menti brillanti che abbiano voglia di crescere con noi. Sarai immerso in un ambiente che valorizza la tua crescita attraverso un training on the job continuo e una formazione specialistica che potenzierà le tue hard e soft skills. La nostra IT & Management Academy ‘Enrico della Valle’ da più di 30 anni crea percorsi di apprendimento innovativi ed esperienziali mirati a valorizzarti, trasmettendoti le conoscenze più richieste sul mercato del lavoro e accompagnandoti nella tua carriera con noi.

We do IT, We teach IT!

SEI L’INNOVAZIONE CHE CERCHIAMO?

Se vuoi trasformare la tua passione in una carriera e riconosci in te queste caratteristiche, non esitare a candidarti:


Hai conseguito o stai conseguendo una laurea in discipline STEM o di Economia;
Consideri il mondo IT e la DIGITAL TRANSFORMATION tra i tuoi interessi principali;
Sei predisposto al lavoro in team e alla condivisione di nuove idee;
Pensi fuori dagli schemi e ti piace creare soluzioni innovative;
Vuoi metterti alla prova, imparare e raggiungere obiettivi ambiziosi.


Invia la tua candidatura e raccontaci come puoi fare la differenza.

SEDI DI INSERIMENTO

Tutte le principali sedi di Italia dove Engineering è presente, come:

Roma | Milano | Torino | Bologna | Palermo | Napoli | Firenze | Genova | Padova

L’annuncio è rivolto a tutti i candidati, senza distinzione di sesso, nel rispetto del Codice delle pari opportunità di cui al D.Lgs. 198/2006 e ss.mm.ii.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Air Apps

At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.

Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.

Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.

The Role

As a Data Analyst at Air Apps, you will be responsible for analyzing and interpreting data to drive business insights and optimize product performance. You will create reports, dashboards, and performance metrics to help teams make informed decisions and improve user experience.

This role requires a data-driven mindset, strong analytical skills, and experience with visualization tools to turn complex data into actionable insights.


This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


Responsibilities


Analyze user behavior, product performance, and key business metrics to identify trends and opportunities.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or Google Data Studio.
Collect, clean, and structure large datasets to ensure accuracy and usability.
Define and track KPIs for marketing, product, and business performance.
Work with SQL, Python, or R to extract, analyze, and manipulate data.
Collaborate with product managers, engineers, and marketing teams to provide data-driven insights.
Conduct A/B testing and experiment analysis to improve product features and user engagement.
Identify bottlenecks and opportunities in user journeys and customer funnels.
Present findings in a clear and compelling way to stakeholders and leadership teams.
Stay up to date with industry trends, analytics best practices, and emerging data tools.


Requirements


Around 3+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field.
Proficiency in SQL for data querying and manipulation.
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Google Data Studio).
Strong analytical skills with experience in Python or R for data analysis.
Knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis.
Experience with Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Amplitude, or similar user analytics tools.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to communicate insights to non-technical stakeholders.
Experience working with large datasets and cloud-based data warehouses (BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake).
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipeline optimization is a plus.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, data-driven environment and manage multiple projects.


What benefits are we offering?


Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
Annual Bonus
Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
Childcare support.
Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
Meals 100% free at the hub.


Diversity & Inclusion

At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.

Application Disclaimer

At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

Compensation Range: €50K - €63K
